data.text:
Hi Jonathan,

I hope you are well.

I have now copied the assignment information into the Assignments tab and linked each assignment with its corresponding rubric. I used the rubrics provided in the Course Details Form and imported them through Kangarubric.

I have also translated the Word documents into HTML and applied them to a consistent Canvas template so the assignment pages are now formatted and published. All three assignments have been placed into their appropriate assignment groups.

One thing to flag, and I may be missing something, but based on the Course Details Form the three assessments add up to 95 percent. Could you please confirm whether there is an additional component, or whether the total weighting is intended to remain at 95 percent?

When you have a moment, please review the[ assignment pages](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/31292/assignments "‌") and the rubrics for accuracy. If you are happy with these, I can replace the existing Word documents in the Graded Assessments module with the updated HTML versions.

Many thanks,

‌

Rich

Kindly review and let me know if there’s anything I may have missed or if you’d like additional points to be included.

Update the indicative learner hours on the course details form

**Subject:** Organic Chemistry 2 quiz bank support?

‌

Dear Richard, 

‌

I have a large library of quiz questions that have been developed by the legacy UoA course that I’m struggling to integrate into the new Canvas page. All the questions are there, but they are missing the image file that is central to each question. 

‌

The question bank resides here: [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/32580/external_tools/205](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/32580/external_tools/205 "‌")

‌

When looking at these questions the required bmp image file is named but does not show up. Those files can be found inside the Files section of the course, but they are not linked to the questions as they are in the original MyUni course. 

‌

Is this something you can help us with?

‌

Kind Regards, 
Thomas

**Subject:** Re: Canvas Shell Structure Implemented for ARTI 6000

‌

Hi Orvila,

In the process of adding content from your PPTX into Topic 2, it’s become clear that creating a separate concept page for each PPTX slide results in too many pages within the Canvas shell. This layout could feel overwhelming for students and make navigation difficult. See below (I will delete these pages, I just wanted to show you first hand)

![Screenshot 2025-11-12 at 3.56.09 pm.png](cid:963755869*7399F661-B28E-492E-BCBC-BFB63972F8DA)

As I’m not the subject matter expert, I can’t determine which PPTX topics could be merged without risking content accuracy, and doing a detailed mapping exercise would be quite time-consuming for both of us.

I’d suggest instead embedding each PPTX on a single concept page, with additional teaching or extended explanations placed on separate concept pages where necessary. This would streamline the student experience while still allowing space for deeper material.

I’ll send you a calendar invite for a quick discussion before 2 pm on Thursday to confirm the best approach.

Kind regards,

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

**Online Programs Team | Learning Enhancement & Innovation**
Division of Academic and Student Engagement

‌

**THE UNIVERSITY OF ADELAIDE**

**Subject:** Canvas Shell Structure Implemented for ARTI 6000

‌

Hi Orvila,

Thank you to both you and Jamie for your time this morning during the handover meeting. As discussed, I’ve now reviewed the Course Details form and have extracted the three modules, ten topics, and the associated key concept areas. I’ve implemented this structure into the Canvas shell.

You can view the draft layout here:[https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/30805/modules](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/30805/modules "‌")

I’ve also attached a screenshot with some brief annotations to explain the approach taken. In particular, please take a moment to review the question highlighted in green regarding the structure for the Key Events section each week.

To confirm, would you prefer:

1. One “Seminar” page per week that contains all discussion points and activities within it, or
2. Individual pages titled according to each specific activity (e.g. demo, walkthrough, case discussion, etc.)?

Your preference here will guide how I finalise the weekly event structure across the semester.

Please let me know your thoughts when you have time. I’m happy to make adjustments and can also work through this together in an online meeting at your next convenience.

If you have time Wednesday or Thursday afternoon please let me know and I can send you a calendar invite. 

Once we have this structure confirmed, you can continue working on the assessment details/rubrics in the Course Details from, then you’ll be ready to start adding content to your course either before or after your working-bee on Friday depending on your schedule.

Kind regards,

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

list.name: Judith - Radiation Biology, Protection and Epidemiology

card.desc:
Hi Judith and Tyron,

I’ve now set up the [assignment pages](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/assignments "‌") in Canvas and aligned the assessment structure with the course outline. The assessment groups currently in place are:

![screencapture-learn-adelaide-edu-au-courses-7567-assignments-2025-11-10-14\_43\_48.png](cid:BDF50D07-E394-49E2-B98B-247608B47FB5)

- **Quiz Portfolio** – 10%
- **Problem Sets** _(Please update this term ‘problem set’ if not appropriate)_ – 40% total (four problem-solving tasks, 10% each)
- **Examination** – 50%

Each "Problem Set" is numbered and titled to match course details form:

      2.1: Radiation sources, risks and biological effects

      2.2: Health effects, shielding and protection

      2.3: Tumour development and modelling

      2.4: Legislation and epidemiology

‌

I’ve also updated the **Course Roadmap** [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/pages/course-roadmap](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/pages/course-roadmap "‌") to show maintaining a two-week turnaround between tasks (as Judith lauded to in our meeting this afternoon), and aligning topic names, forums etc with the module structure in purple.

![screencapture-learn-adelaide-edu-au-courses-7567-pages-course-roadmap-2025-11-10-14\_55\_23.png](cid:341986953*253C182B-42E6-4B6F-9AF3-06FACF55AE9C)

‌

When you have a chance, could you please:

1. Review individual each **assignment page** for wording and clarity. \@Tyron Turnbull you can open each assignment by clicking on each page here: [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/assignments](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/assignments "‌")
   1. I’ve left some red highlights on the the pages for one of you to please edit and update. If the wording is not in sync with your expectations please feel free to change entirely. 
   1. ![Screenshot 2025-11-10 at 3.04.02 pm.png](cid:341986953*74BC3AE7-DCE2-4249-BD79-7E1748ED0DBD)
2. Confirm that the **grouping and weightings** match your expectations.
3. Review the **Course Roadmap** and make any adjustments you’d like.

Hopefully this is helpful and 80-90% of the way there in terms of correct wording, due dates, percentages etc. I imagine it’s not perfect so please do take the take to go through the pages and edit them directly and this should be fairly easy. If you need help with editing these page in Canvas please don’t hesitate to reach out

Thanks,

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

**Online Programs Team | Learning Enhancement & Innovation**
Division of Academic and Student Engagement

card.desc:
HI Tracey,

‌

No worries, I’ve swept your course up to the end of topic 3 and uplifted some of the pages as requested.

‌

I’ve also left “@comments” in a yellow highlighted colour for you to review and respond to please. If you could please let me know your response to these questions either on the page in canvas or via email reply.

I’ll also add some notes next to links on pages below just as an FYI where no further changes are necessarily required.

‌

1. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/the-science-of-climate-change-causes-and-impacts?module\_item\_id=80062e](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/the-science-of-climate-change-causes-and-impacts?module_item_id=80062e "‌")
2. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/key-greenhouse-gases-ghgs-and-their-sources?module\_item\_id=291649](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/key-greenhouse-gases-ghgs-and-their-sources?module_item_id=291649 "‌")
3. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/systems-thinking-uncertainty-and-climate-change](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/systems-thinking-uncertainty-and-climate-change "‌") - **This page had quotes as images, which in terms of accessibility requirements is not best practice, so I have converted them to text and retained everything else on the page.**
4. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/topic-summary](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/topic-summary "‌") - **I’ve updated the styling of the headings and summary boxes on pages like this to use the suggested conventions and CidiLabs style boxes.**
5. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/seminar-outline-for-activity-13](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/seminar-outline-for-activity-13 "‌") **There is a broken link on this page** **I imagine you would like to fix**
6. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/carbon-reduction-targets-and-timelines](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/carbon-reduction-targets-and-timelines "‌") **I cleaned up the heading types and colours on this page**

‌

‌

I have enabled the new Dynamic Home Page for you: [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185 "‌") and added this banner image.

‌

Please let me know if you prefer something else:

‌

![Image](cid:3705452723*image005.png@01DC44E3.A7ED8B60)

‌

‌

Also, you will need to reach out to the Library support team to request a reading list is created for your shell: [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/external_tools/216](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/external_tools/216 "‌")

‌

‌

I tried to do this for you however the system won’t save once I create a new list from scratch so there must be a bug the library can help resolve: [library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌")

‌

Once the list is created, I can ask for the media team to help populate it based off the links in the course

‌

‌

![Image](cid:3705452723*image004.png@01DC44DE.D1AAC350)

‌

‌

I will continue uplift pages for you next week, taking up from where I left off today ([here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2185/pages/seminar-outline-2?module_item_id=345060 "‌") for the record)

Have a great weekend!

‌

‌

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

Division of Academic and Student Engagement

card.desc:
Dear Thomas, Anton & Simon,

‌

As you are aware, **Organic Chemistry 2** has been scheduled for additional development between from **8 September 2025 to 14 November 2025** Following on from the peer review and finalisation period, the following items are to be completed by the end of this additional development period: 

‌

- **CDF and assessment completed** 
- **Rework on front landing page** 
- **Development of video contents** 
- **Contents development for topic 2-6**
- **Build course readings**
- **Restructuring on assessment grouping**

‌

As your Project Officer, I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss progress. 

‌

**Additional Development Discussion** 

Week 1: 9 September 2025 

**Milestone 1 check-in** 

Week 4: 1 October 2025 

**Milestone 2 check-in** 

Week 7: 21 October 2025 

**Milestone 3 check-in** 

Week 10: 12 November 2025 

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_ 

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

The TALAS support available during this additional development period is outlined below. The timeline of support is the same as standard course development, but the time allocation has reduced. 

‌

**Role** 

**Name** 

**Email** 

 **Support Provided** 

 **Time Allocation** 

**Available Timeframe** 

**Learning Designer** 

‌

**(LD)** 

Richard Bartlett 

[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌") 

Provides advice and support in the conceptualisation, design, and sequencing of course content and learning activities. They can help you scaffold learning activities toward assessment, scope and design the development of learning content, and provide advice and support in the effective use of technology in learning and teaching. 

‌

7\.5 hours 

**Weeks 4 to 8:** 

‌

Monday 29 September – Friday 31 October 

‌

\*Please note, both your CED & LD are available during week 4 

**Course Build Support** 

Your Learning Designer will arrange course build support 

Builds some content in the LMS; completion of fundamental ad-hoc design tasks. 

‌

10 hours 

**Weeks 6 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 13 October – Friday 14 November 

‌

**Media Design** 

\-Email [jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au](mailto:jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au "‌") to schedule your consult 

 
More information [here.](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/media-2 "‌") 

Provides advice and support in selecting types of multimedia for your course, exploring options for filming videos, scheduling filming or media development, and designing bespoke media assets. 

‌

2\.5 hours 

‌

**Weeks 4 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 29 September – Friday 14 November 

**Media Production** 

Your consult with Media Design will guide any next steps 

Supports developers to produce engaging media content (through their team of skilled multimedia designers, filmmakers, animators, recording studios, self-serve facilities and equipment).    

7\.5 hours 

**Weeks 6 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 13 October – Friday 14 November 

‌

**Library** 

(course readings and resource selection) 

[library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌") 

Support in identifying resources that align with your course. Copyright guidance. 

As needed 

**Recommended in Weeks 2 to 4:** 

‌

Monday 15 September – Friday 3 October  

**Academic Transition Panel**  

**(ATP)** 

Andrew MacKinnon (UoA), Emma Sherratt (UoA), Tom Raimondo (UniSA), & Craig Williams (UniSA) 

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development. 

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns.  

‌

‌

‌

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link**.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/749 "‌") 

**Course Development Journey** 

Outlines the key activities across the 10 weeks. 

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/key-information "‌") 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

The main hub for course development information. Provides guidance on the design, development, and build phases. 

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370 "‌") 

‌

**SharePoint Folder** 

The dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members.  

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209 "‌") 

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Find out more about the library [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/library?module_item_id=665199 "‌") 

‌

You can contact the library at [library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌") 

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

The Course Design workshops will be held on the following dates: 

- Week 1: Thursday 1pm - 3pm  

- Week 1: Friday 10am - 12pm 

‌

Register for the Course Design workshop [here](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/t0uWCP7LD9SKMRnp3I0hytx-g7G?domain=events.humanitix.com "‌") 

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops-2 "‌") 

‌

I look forward to seeing you at our _Additional Development Discussion_ meeting.  

‌

‌

Kind Regards,

‌

**Eileen Cheong** (she/her)

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

card.desc:
Dear Jonathan, 

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **Negotiation** course, scheduled for development from **13 October 2025 to 19 December 2025.** 

‌

My name is Sophia Carson, I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and I am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below).  

‌

As this course is scheduled for delivery in **Semester 1**, our development period runs **right up to delivery**, with limited time between finalisation and teaching. Please take note of the milestones below - these will help us stay on track so that, by the end of this 10-week period, your course is **ready for review and as close to ready to teach as possible**. Between the later milestones, I’ll also check in via email to monitor progress and provide support as needed. 

‌

**Course kick-off** 

Week 1: w/c 13 October 2025 

**Milestone 1 check-in:**  

- Course Details Form populated 
- Commenced learning content (draft) 
- Commenced media and build planning (draft) 

Week 4: w/c 3 November 2025 

**Milestone 2 check-in:**  

- Canvas built out with relevant modules and topics   
- Completed Learning content and semi-populated in Canvas   
- Assessments built in Canvas 
- Scripts / recordings for videos 
- Visual assets  (H5Ps, images, animations etc.) 

Week 7: w/c 24 November 2025 

**Milestone 3 check-in:**  

- Completed Course Details Form, populate CurV record & export. 
- Canvas course with relevant modules and topics   
- Finalised all learning content   
- Assessments built in Canvas 
- Activities and media embedded   

Week 10: w/c 15 December 2025 

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_ 

‌

![Clapper board with solid fill, Picture](cid:71299617*fbd7ff4d-bb40-4614-980c-31fcecd794fc)

**Prior to our course kick-off meeting**  

Please ensure that you watch the 5-minute [**Course Development Overview Video**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/program-and-course-overview "‌"). This video provides an overview of what to expect over the next 10 weeks, including the support and resources available to you. Please come to the kick-off meeting with any questions, or feel free to reach out to me beforehand.  

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below: 

‌

Paul Moss 

[paul.moss@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:paul.moss@adelaide.edu.au "‌")

5 hours

‌

Monday 13 October – Friday 7 November 

‌

They can help you scaffold learning activities toward assessment, scope and design the development of learning content, and provide advice and support in the effective use of technology in learning and teaching. 

‌

15 hours

Monday 3 November – Friday 5 December

\*Please note, both your CED & LD are available during week 4

20 hours

Monday 17 November – Friday 19 December

Provides advice and support in selecting types of multimedia for your course, exploring options for filming videos, scheduling filming or media development, and designing bespoke media assets. 

‌

5 hours

As needed

**Academic Transition Panel**  

**(ATP)** 

Mahmud Masum (UoA) & Lan Snell (UniSA) 

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development. 

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns.  

‌

**Curriculum Quality Panel**

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link on myLearning** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link.](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/27618 "‌") 

**Curriculum Management System (CurV)** 

You have been granted the role of Designer in the Curriculum Management System, CurV.  

‌

Once you have finalised your Course Details Form you will use this to populate your course’s CurV record by the end of your 10-week development cycle. 

‌

Please use your a-number and password to log into CurV: [https://curriculum.adelaide.edu.au/cms](https://curriculum.adelaide.edu.au/cms "‌")   

‌

A Quick Reference Guide and video run through is available [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-details-form-slash-curv-population?module_item_id=665215 "‌"). 

‌

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins. 

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/key-information "‌") 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370 "‌") 

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Learning Centre Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Learning Centre Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209 "‌") 

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Find out more about the library [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/library?module_item_id=665199 "‌") 

‌

You can contact the library at [library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌")  

**Course Design Workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

‌

The Course Design workshops will be held on the following dates: 

- Week 1: Thursday 1pm - 3pm  
- Week 1: Friday 10am - 12pm 

‌

Register for the Course Design workshop [here](https://events.humanitix.com/designing-your-course-and-assessments-workshop-online "‌") 

‌

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops-2 "‌") 

**Next Steps:**  

Next week is the first week of development and I encourage you to book in consults with your CED and continue thinking about your course design, you may wish to start populating the [Course Details Form](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-details-form-slash-curv-population?module_item_id=665215 "‌"). 

‌

I look forward to seeing you at our kick-off meeting.  

‌

Kind regards, 

**Sophia Carson**  

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

‌

**Integration Management Office (IMO)**

card.desc:
Dear Judith, 

‌

Welcome to the official development period for **Radiation Biology, Protection and Epidemiology** course, scheduled for development from **8 September 2025** to **14 November 2025**. This course has been selected by the Academic Transition Panel (ATP) for at-elbow support. 

‌

I am sending this on behalf of Vy Gubatina, your Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and she is your key contact during course development. Vy’s support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. She will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones. 

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link**.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567 "‌") 

**Curriculum Management System (CurV)** 

You have been granted the role of Designer in the Curriculum Management System, CurV.  

‌

Once you have finalised your Course Details Form you will use this to populate your course’s CurV record by the end of your 10-week development cycle. 

‌

Please use your a-number and password to log into CurV: [https://curriculum.adelaide.edu.au/cms](https://curriculum.adelaide.edu.au/cms "smartCard-inline")    

‌

A Quick Reference Guide and video run through is available [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-details-form-slash-curv-population?module_item_id=665215 "‌"). 

‌

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins.  

Attached document 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

‌

As you are receiving dedicated support via the At Elbow model, the hours for TALAS support and consult booking section within the Essential Site are not applicable to you. 

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370 "‌") 

‌

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") 

 (you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions andworkshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209 "‌") 

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Find out more about the library [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/library?module_item_id=665199 "‌") 

‌

You can contact the library at [library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌")  

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

‌

The Course Design workshops will be held on the following dates: 

- Week 1: Thursday 1pm - 3pm  

- Week 1: Friday 10am - 12pm 

‌

Register for the Course Design workshop [here](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/6unLCOMKB2ip4jGEWtEf7tGfamn?domain=events.humanitix.com "‌") 

‌

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops-2 "‌") 

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, who will support you throughout the 10 weeks. We will meet regularly together and you are also encouraged to meet outside of this as well to progress work. 

‌

**Role** 

**Name** 

**Contact Details** 

**Support Provided** 

**Time Allocation** 

**Curriculum & Education Developer**  

**(CED)** 

Sarah Davey

[Sarah.Davey@unisa.edu.au](mailto:Sarah.Davey@unisa.edu.au "‌")

CEDs provide pedagogical advice and support in curriculum andassessment design. This includes reviewing and providing feedback on course, assignment, and rubric design. 

10 

**Learning Designer**  

**(LD)** 

Richard Bartlett

[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌")  

Provides advice and support in the conceptualisation, design, andsequencing of course content andlearning activities. They can help you scaffold learning activities toward assessment, scope anddesign the development of learning content, and provide advice andsupport in the effective use of technology in learning andteaching. 

‌

20 

**Digital Education Developer (DED)** 

Gulshan Gupta

[gulshan.gupta@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:gulshan.gupta@adelaide.edu.au "‌")

Provides build consultation andspecialist learning experience advice. Contributes technical build support to the At-Elbow team including; development of key learning assets, setup of enterprise external tool assessments, alignment of content with accessibility and usability standards, and population into Canvas. DEDs also develop design briefs in consultation with TALAS Media. 

‌

30 

**Media Design** 

‌

Jeremy Bowtell 

[jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au](mailto:jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au "‌") 

Provides advice and support in selecting types of multimedia for your course, exploring options for filming videos, scheduling filming or media development, and designing bespoke media assets. 

‌

10 

‌

**Media Production** 

‌

Jeremy Bowtell 

[jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au](mailto:jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au "‌") 

Supports developers to produce engaging media content (through their team of skilled multimedia designers, filmmakers, animators, recording studios, self-serve facilities and equipment).    

20 

‌

**Library** 

(course readings andresource selection) 

[library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌") 

Support in identifying resources that align with your course. Copyright guidance. 

‌

‌

As needed 

**Academic Transition Panel**  

**(ATP)** 

Andrew MacKinnon (UoA), Emma Sherratt (UoA), Tom Raimondo (UniSA), & Craig Williams (UniSA)

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development. 

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns. 

‌

‌

You can continue with the design work you have been doing. We look forward to seeing you at our first meeting next week. You may wish to contact your CED to organise a time asap next week.   

‌

‌

Kind Regards,

‌

**Eileen Cheong** (she/her) on behalf of **Vy Gubatina** 

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

‌

**Integration Management Office (IMO)** 

Level 3, 108 North Terrace, Adelaide SA 5000 
E: [eileen.cheong@unisa.edu.au](mailto:eileen.cheong@unisa.edu.au "‌")

Following up from this morning’s meeting: I’ve set up a first pass of the five assignments (plus the exam) in your Canvas shell. You can view them here:

card.desc:
### Structure and modules

- Four modules confirmed as acceptable, to be recorded in the Course Details Form.
- Assessment tasks aligned with modules, already completed.

### Assessment and rubrics

- Course rubric discussed, with example rubrics provided for consideration.

### Content and learning resources

- Content migration into the course underway, with support expected from the Course Builders team.
- Media team support flagged for visual/statistical content (e.g. species data), with Will to follow up with Georgia Forrest.
- Additional project officer items (H5P build, video content, content migration, reworked landing page) appear to be covered within the agreed tasks.

### Course design and homepage

- Homepage: Will to adapt to the standard template for now.
- Noted that a new dynamic homepage will be available soon, which should enhance the student experience.

### Assessment delivery

- Invigilated online exams under investigation, with Simon and Rich to look at Examsoft and question bank options.

---

**In summary:**
The major elements of course structure, assessment alignment, and rubrics are in place. Content migration and media development are progressing with support from Course Builders and Media. The homepage is being worked on to fit the standard template until the dynamic version is released. Outstanding items include confirming invigilated exam options and continuing resource development (H5P, videos).

card.desc:
Hi Bonnie,

Quick update: I have a basic **Google Form + QR code** setup which live and working as intended.

- Students enter their name and email, submit, and are **automatically allocated a topic**.
- The script then **emails each student** their allocated topic immediately.
- I’ve set **it so the script for** each topic has a fixed capacity (currently 30). Once a topic fills, it’s no longer assigned.
- All allocations are visible in the **linked Google Sheet** for academic oversight.

Links for testing:

- Form: [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSclqRhjO2vq5aOOVQLzTo1ywwuf8Oi1tJXj-K5jMUKOWypn8A/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=106757145152036440538](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSclqRhjO2vq5aOOVQLzTo1ywwuf8Oi1tJXj-K5jMUKOWypn8A/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=106757145152036440538&utm_source=chatgpt.com "‌")
- Responses sheet (live log): [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WJzkIdNzNV9ZsUOa76XvcU5ClM\_weN5bq\_fqa8G0jkI/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WJzkIdNzNV9ZsUOa76XvcU5ClM_weN5bq_fqa8G0jkI/edit?usp=sharing&utm_source=chatgpt.com "‌")

I’ve also included the **QR code** so you can test it. Functionally, if this meets the need, we can uplift the wording and visual style next.

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

‌

Thanks for you time again this morning.

- Just confirming that Assignment 1 will be a single submission. We’ll ensure the excess assignment pages are removed, and that the rubrics and related elements are attached to the uplifted assignment pages Rich suggested.

Zofia will continue working through the uplift of Topics 1–3. Both Rich and Zofia will add comments and highlights directly in Canvas. Ron and Ari, you’ve indicated you’re happy to collaborate on this process by reviewing comments, making/accepting changes, and finalising edits to the pages.

Thanks all,

card.name: AFW Honours Handbook – H5P Interactive Book prototype

list.name: Honours Plant, Food and Soil Sciences Project

card.desc:
**Summary of engagement:**

- Cristian Varela (Honours Director) and Han Weng contacted me about converting the _Honours Handbook_ PDF into an interactive H5P object for AU Learn, to be used in both the _Advanced Plant, Food and Soil Sciences_ and _Honours Plant, Food and Soil Sciences Project_ courses.
- Key requirement: exclude all timetable dates (updated every 6 months) and retain the rest of the content.

**What’s being asked:**

- Provide a concept and prototype for an interactive version of the handbook in H5P, suitable for embedding in both courses.
- Recommend appropriate H5P content types to make the handbook engaging and easy to navigate.

**What I’ve done so far:**

- Reviewed the PDF and extracted headings and content (excluding timetable dates).
- Created an **H5P Interactive Book** mock-up in my sandpit with the handbook structure and content copied verbatim from the PDF.
- Developed a **conversion plan** outlining potential H5P content types for each section (e.g., Information Wall, Accordion, Timeline, Documentation Tool).
- Added Cristian to my sandpit for review and feedback.
- Offered to meet via Teams (tomorrow, Friday morning, or next week) to discuss direction and refinements.

card.desc:
Hi Tarikul.

‌

Happy Monday and hope you had a great weekend!

‌

I have been through your course, right through from Topic 1 to 10. When you have time you may wish to review my suggestions. If you’re not content with the changes the options are:

‌

1. Easily revert back to your last edit via “page history” and restore the page with no uplifts implemented.
2. If you deem the page is an absolute improvement then no changes required
3. If you consider a page a _slight_ improvement however you believe edits are required
   1. This will require you to go on to the page, click edit and make the changes. 

‌

The suggested uplifts I’ve implemented are mainly aesthetic such as wrapping information in direction boxes, or providing framing text for videos, activities. Also fixing US spelling to AU/British.

‌

I’ve will add PDF version of this reflections pages so students can download it and print. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/course-concepts-and-reflections-summary-table](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/course-concepts-and-reflections-summary-table "‌")

**Please note:** Unfortunately, there is no further scope or time unfortunately to further edit the content pages. Please make any final refinements as you see fit.

‌

That said, there are some easy and specific fixes I’m happy to help fix if you can provide the supporting material:

Dear Ari, Ron and Dan,

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **Thinking By Design** course, scheduled for development from **4 August - 10 October 2025.** This course has been selected by the Academic Transition Panel (ATP) for at-elbow support. 

‌

My name is Meg Madden and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones. 

‌

Prior to our course kick-off meeting

![Clapper board with solid fill](cid:2216622259*image002.png@01DC0166.3500F760)

Please ensure that you watch the 5-minute [**Course Development Overview Video**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/before-you-start-2 "‌"). This video provides an overview of what to expect over the next 10 weeks, including the support and resources available to you. Please come to the kick-off meeting with any questions, or feel free to reach out to me beforehand.  

‌

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content.

Attached document

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. **Key Contacts:**

Meg Madden

card.name: Analytical and Sustainable Chemistry 2

card.desc:
Hi Joe and Anna,

‌

Thanks again for your time this morning.

‌

After you left the meeting, Joe, Anna and I made a few small tweaks to the titles on the graphic to improve clarity and alignment, which Anna will also email you about. We then discussed the idea of uplifting this visual into a short animated video with voiceover to step students through the decision-making process. The goal would be to provide a clear and engaging asynchronous resource that demystifies the pathways available for review.

‌

I’ve used AI to flesh out a rough outline and draft script that shows how either one of you—or someone else—could narrate the content. It’s designed to closely follow the visual structure of the graphic and frame the learning experience in a clear and student-friendly way.

‌

Please let me know if it’s done a somewhat accurate job in capturing how you would create a such a script/structure. If you’d like to go down this path, please make changes or annotations that would help bring it into alignment with what’s needed to ensure it’s comprehensive and accurate from a content perspective.

‌

Looking forward to your thoughts.

‌

‌

‌

‌

![Image](cid:4036917002*image002.png@01DBEA6F.85010E70)

‌

Kindly,

‌

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

**Online Programs Team | Learning Enhancement & Innovation**
Division of Academic and Student Engagement

‌

**THE UNIVERSITY OF ADELAIDE**

old.desc:
2\.5 HRS

Hi Anna and Joe,

Thanks for this extra info and pointing me in the right direction to the content in your sharepoint.

I’ve created prototype module sequences for both ‘**Merits Review’** and ‘**Procedural Fairness’**. These include structured concept pages, sample interactive elements, and APA-style reference formatting at the bottom of each page to give you a sense of how a topic design could function.

‌

‌

‌

‌

‌

‌

‌

I've referred to the course details document, lecture slides and materials you've shared and used AI prompting in some instances to write HTML code and help structure and scaffold each topic. Please note, the name of the **concept pages** and the **text** currently on the pages is ‘dummy content’ — think of it like lorem ipsum, except more relevant to the subject. While it may resemble what you intend to include, the final wording will need to come from you both. I’ve used this placeholder content to maintain coherence and avoid confusion from irrelevant subject matter.

‌

I’ve also embedded some images from your lecture slides that may or may not be reused — we can flag later if you’d like any of them recreated or uplifted by the media team.

‌

The purpose of this prototype is to:

- Show my interpretation of good practice in instructional design
- Demonstrate the use of H5P interactives and DesignPlus callout boxes
- Provide a consistent and accessible student experience through logical sequencing and academic styling

‌

Please treat this as a foundation for discussion when we meet next week. If anything looks off or isn’t what you had in mind, please let me know as soon as possible so I can make the necessary corrections early.

‌

You can start exploring the modules here:

‌

- Merits Review module:
  [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582/pages/module-overview-5?module\_item\_id=581505](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582/pages/module-overview-5?module_item_id=581505 "‌")
- Procedural Fairness module:
  [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582/pages/module-overview-2?module\_item\_id=581500](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582/pages/module-overview-2?module_item_id=581500 "‌")

Just use the **‘Next’** button in the bottom-right corner of each page to step through the full sequence.

**Final note:** After our meeting next week, my job will be to set up the concept page titles based on your advice and direction, and I’m happy to continue structuring the pages accordingly. The idea is to set you both up for success by creating a “paint-by-numbers” framework — once that’s in place, I’ll work behind the scenes to uplift other content, create the H5P interactives, and liaise with the media team. I just wanted to be clear that you’ll need to guide me and map the actual learning content.

Looking forward to your feedback.

Kindly,

**Rich Bartlett**

card.desc:
Hi Tarikul,

‌

Thanks for your time this afternoon.

‌

After we got off the call, I starting mapping the Module and topics named to the canvas module boxes. But I hit a road block for week 2 & 3 because it seems as thought you’ve put multiple _**concept**_ title pages into the cell instead of just a single _**topic** name_**.**

As you know the naming convention is module name : topic name with subpages nested underneath being “concepts”

‌

With topic 2 for example, I’ve created all the items in that cell table from the course details document as concept pages.

‌

‌

‌

If you could please let me know what you would like topic/week 2 and 3 to be named I can update this.

‌

Further to this Tarikul, do you have the names/order of the concepts pages prepared for each topic/week?

‌

‌

‌

I have also replicated the structure for the Module Overview pages from Global Business Analysis

‌

‌

I’ll wait to hear from you.

‌

Kindly,
card.name: Created course structure

card.desc:
Hi Sophia,

‌

Thanks for forwarding this through.

‌

I’m happy to assist where I can, but just to flag—writing rubrics from scratch sits outside of my role as a Learning Designer. That said, I noticed the Canvas shell already includes rubric information attached to the assignments under Imported Assignments. Would Monica, Carl, or Stijn like me to copy and paste that rubric content into the Course Details form? I’m happy to assist with that admin task if it helps move things forward (see screenshot attached).

‌

I've also grouped the assignments into three categories—**Research Proposal, Research Report, and Research Thesis**—based on the milestone timeline graphic. However, I’m not entirely confident that each assignment has been placed in the most appropriate group, so it would be helpful if Monica or someone familiar with the assessment structure could review and confirm in Canvas (also see attached screenshot/s).

‌

Just to gently note, it is the Course Builder’s responsibility to complete the Course Details form, so if no edits have been made yet, there’s only so much I can do at this stage from a Learning Design perspective.

‌

Happy to help further where appropriate—just let me know.

Kindly,

Hi Anna and Joe,

Thanks for this update Eliani’s, I just wanted to touch base with a few updates and next steps.

I’ve updated the Canvas home page so that the full module and topic structure now appears in line with the 10-week teaching breakdown—great to see that this structure has already been mapped out. If you're happy with the banner image and updated icons used for each module box, feel free to let me know. I'm happy to make changes if you have any preferences or suggestions.

‌

‌

‌

[\@Joe](mailto:Joe.McIntyre@unisa.edu.au "‌") – once we're ready to meet, it would be helpful to clarify the purpose of the two folders in SharePoint titled Substantive Content and UniSA Source Materials. There seems to be some crossover between the two in terms of lecture slides and other resources, so I’d like to get a clearer sense of which is the source of truth and whether we can consolidate or clean up these files.

‌

‌

‌

Looking ahead—do you intend to rebrand the PowerPoint lecture slides using University of Adelaide branding? I’m happy to get that process started with the Media team if the content is ready, but I can also hold off if further updates to teaching materials are expected.

Once you’ve finalised the assessment details and rubrics in the Course Details form, I can also help build these assessment items directly into your Canvas course shell. If you need another week or two to complete this step, that’s absolutely fine—there’s still time.

card.desc:
Dear Joe, Anna, and Kylie,

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **Administrative Law** course, scheduled for development from **26 May 2025** to **1 August 2025**.

‌

My name is **Sally Kuzniecow** and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below). 

‌

**Course kick-off**

Week 1: w/c 26 May 2025

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design

Week 4: w/c 16 June 2025

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development

Week 7: w/c 7 July 2025

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development Completion

Week 10: w/c 28 July 2025

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources**

Resource

Description

Link

**Course Shell Link**

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content.

[Course Shell Link**.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582 "‌")

**Course Development Journey**

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins.

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌")

**AU Course Development Essentials Site**

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete. 

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌")

**SharePoint Folder**

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work.

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name)

‌

**Training & Support**

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources.

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌")

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool. 

Book an appointment [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-and-booking-consultations "‌")

‌

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments.

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌")

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building.

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌")

‌

**Key Contacts:**

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below:

‌

**Role**

**Name**

**Email**

**Support Provided**

**Time Allocation**

**Available Timeframe**

**Curriculum & Education Developer**

‌

**(CED)**

Eliani Boton

[Eliani.Boton@unisa.edu.au](mailto:Eliani.Boton@unisa.edu.au "‌")

Provides curriculum and pedagogical advice and support including course planning, assessments, course overview, and high-level module/topic structure. 

5 hours

‌

**Weeks 1 to 4:**

‌

Monday 26 May – Friday 20 June

**Learning Designer**

‌

**(LD)**

Richard Bartlett

[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌")

Contributes to the identification and design of media and learning activities (for face-to-face and online formats). They may also help to extend the course plan.

15 hours

**Weeks 4 to 8:**

‌

Monday 16 June – Friday 18 July

‌

\*Please note, both your CED & LD are available during week 4

**Course Build Support**

Your Learning Designer will arrange course build support

Builds some content in the LMS; completion of fundamental ad-hoc design tasks.

‌

20 hours

**Weeks 6 to 10:**

‌

Monday 30 June – Friday 1 August

‌

**Media Design**

\-Email [mediaproduction@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:mediaproduction@adelaide.edu.au "‌") to schedule your consult

‌

More information [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-booking-consultations-and-workshops#mediaContent "‌") and a useful [help guide](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/designing-your-media "‌")

Provides guidance on selecting the best types of media for courses and assists with preparing for the development of a range of media types.

5 hours

‌

**Weeks 4 to 10:**

‌

Monday 16 June – Friday 1 August

**Media Production**

Your consult with Media Design will guide any next steps

Supports developers to produce media content (through their team of skilled multimedia designers, professional recording studios, and self-serve facilities and equipment).

15 hours

**Weeks 6 to 10:**

‌

Monday 30 June – Friday 1 August

‌

**Library**

(course readings and resource selection)

[library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌")

Support in identifying resources that align with your course. Copyright guidance.

As needed

**Recommended in Weeks 2 to 4:**

‌

Monday 2 June – Friday 20 June 

**Academic Transition Panel** 

**(ATP)**

Judith McNamara (UoA) & Vicki Waye (UniSA)

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development.

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns. 

‌

‌

‌

**Next Steps:** 

Next week is the first week of development and I encourage you to **b**ook in consults with your CED and continue thinking about your course design, you may wish to start populating the [Course Details Form](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/about-the-design-phase "‌").

‌

I look forward to seeing you at our kick-off meeting. 

‌

Kindest regards,

Sally

‌

**Sally Kuzniecow** (she/her)

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

‌

card.desc:
Hi Monica,

Thanks again for your detailed feedback and context around the Canvas structure and timeline image. Based on your notes, here’s the draft email I’m planning to send to the media team (along with Simon’s image) to kick off the request on your behalf.

Could you please take a moment to review and let me know if it captures everything correctly or if anything needs adjustment before I submit the formal ticket?

---

_**Draft email to media team:**_

I’m reaching out regarding the creation of a timeline image for the _Georesources Industry Project_ course (MSc level), based on direction and feedback from the academic team (Monica, Simon, and Stijn).

I’ve attached a **hand-drawn reference image from Simon (Course Builder)** to help guide the general layout and structure. Please note, some of the information in the sketch is no longer current—so please refer to the notes below for accuracy.

### Key instructions and updates:

- **Updated timeframe**
  The course runs from **July to November** (Semester 2). The original sketch shows February to November—please adjust the timeline to reflect this updated semester span.
- **Consistent naming**
  The timeline should use **the same titles as the Canvas module groups**:
  - _Research Proposal_
  - _Research Report_
  - _Research Communication_
    These align with the “activity waves” shown in the hand-drawn version, so please match the wording accordingly for student clarity.
- **Visual structure**
  The academic team would like the image to include the following elements:
  - **A horizontal timeline** from July to November with reference points around mid-August, September, October, and November
  - **Three overlapping phases**, shown as stylised curves or waves, labelled: _Reading/Lit Review_, _Research/Data_, and _Writing/Editing_
  - **Assessment points**: Place markers for _Poster_, _Proposal_, _Thesis_, and _Presentation_ at appropriate spots along the timeline \@Monica Jimenez Lloreda Can you please clarify for me if it is clear where exactly the assessment points will be on the image?
  - **Canvas alignment**: Include horizontal blocks or tabs underneath the timeline reflecting the module groupings listed above
  - **Parallel course bands**: Add thin horizontal strips to show when _Geoscience Communication_ and _Advanced Field Geoscience_ run concurrently
  - **Supervisor meetings**: Represent fortnightly check-ins with repeating markers (e.g. dots, flags, or similar)
- **Design tone and fidelity**
  At this stage, the team is looking for a **basic wireframe or draft layout**. Once that’s ready, they’ll provide feedback to refine emphasis, and structure but please use AU style guide colours and branding. If you could please let me know approx timeframe to create a basic wireframe so I can relay this back to the CBs.

‌

‌

---

‌

**Next step:**

Once I receive confirmation from you that the brief is clear for the media team, I’ll submit a formal ticket through the project management tool and request a timeline for the first iteration. I won’t be able to confirm dates or delivery timeframes until that ticket is submitted and triaged. (As you can see I’ve asked for clarification in my message)

Please let me know about the part highlighted in yellow and if you'd like me to make any adjustments to this before I send it out.

‌

Kind regards,
card.name: Created brief for media team

card.desc:
Dear Tarikul, and Chanaka,

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **International Business Environment** course, scheduled for development from **26 May 2025** to **1 August 2025**. 

‌

My name is Clare Reuther and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below).  

‌

**Course kick-off** 

Week 1: w/c 26 May 2025 

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design 

Week 4: w/c 16 June 2025 

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development 

Week 7: w/c 7 July 2025 

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development Completion 

Week 10: w/c 28 July 2025 

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_ 

‌

‌

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link**.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800 "‌") 

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins. 

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌") 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌") 

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌")(you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌") 

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Book an appointment [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-and-booking-consultations "‌") 

‌

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌") 

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌") 

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below: 

‌

**Role** 

**Name** 

**Email** 

 **Support Provided** 

 **Time Allocation** 

**Available Timeframe** 

**Curriculum & Education Developer** 

‌

**(CED)** 

Kezia Perry 

[kezia.perry@unisa.edu.au](mailto:kezia.perry@unisa.edu.au "‌") 

Provides curriculum and pedagogical advice and support including course planning, assessments, course overview, and high-level module/topic structure.  

5 hours 

‌

**Weeks 1 to 4:** 

‌

Monday 26 May – Friday 20 June 

**Learning Designer** 

‌

**(LD)** 

Richard Bartlett 

[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌") 

Contributes to the identification and design of media and learning activities (for face-to-face and online formats). They may also help to extend the course plan. 

15 hours 

**Weeks 4 to 8:** 

‌

Monday 16 June – Friday 18 July 

‌

\*Please note, both your CED & LD are available during week 4 

**Course Build Support** 

Your Learning Designer will arrange course build support 

Builds some content in the LMS; completion of fundamental ad-hoc design tasks. 

‌

20 hours 

**Weeks 6 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 30 June – Friday 1 August 

‌

**Media Design** 

\-Email [mediaproduction@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:mediaproduction@adelaide.edu.au "‌") to schedule your consult 

 
More information [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-booking-consultations-and-workshops#mediaContent "‌") and a useful [help guide](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/designing-your-media "‌") 

Provides guidance on selecting the best types of media for courses and assists with preparing for the development of a range of media types. 

5 hours 

‌

**Weeks 4 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 16 June – Friday 1 August 

**Media Production** 

Your consult with Media Design will guide any next steps 

Supports developers to produce media content (through their team of skilled multimedia designers, professional recording studios, and self-serve facilities and equipment). 

15 hours 

**Weeks 6 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 30 June – Friday 1 August 

‌

**Library** 

(course readings and resource selection) 

[library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌") 

Support in identifying resources that align with your course. Copyright guidance. 

As needed 

**Recommended in Weeks 2 to 4:** 

‌

Monday 2 June – Friday 20 June  

**Academic Transition Panel**  

**(ATP)** 

Hussain Rammal (UoA) & You-il Lee (UniSA) 

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development. 

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns.  

‌

‌

‌

‌

**Next Steps:**  

Next week is the first week of development and I encourage you to **b**ook in consults with your CED and continue thinking about your course design, you may wish to start populating the [Course Details Form](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/about-the-design-phase "‌"). 

‌

I look forward to seeing you at our kick-off meeting.  

‌

Kind regards, 

Clare Reuther 

‌

**Clare Reuther** (she/her)

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

card.desc:
elcome to the official development period for **Medical Radiation Physics Research Project A / Medical Radiation Physics Research Project B** course, scheduled for development from **21 April 2025** to **27 June 2025**. 

‌

As I mentioned at the discipline kick-off meeting a couple of weeks ago, I am your Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below).  

‌

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design 

Week 1: w/c 21 April 2025 

_Please note that the kick-off meeting may be brought forward to the week of 14 April due to the Easter and ANZAC Day public holidays and potential leave which people may have taken during this time._    

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development 

Week 4: w/c 12 May 2025 

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development 

Week 7: w/c 2 June 2025 

**Milestone 4 check-in:** Course Development Completion 

Week 10: w/c 23 June 2025 

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_ 

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[**Course Shell Link.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4759 "‌") 

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins. 

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌") 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌") 

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌")  

(you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌") 

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Book an appointment [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-and-booking-consultations "‌") 

‌

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

[1pm - 3pm 24 April](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/FFkcCq714rS8RWov3SQiVfEMhUh?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌") 

[10am - 12pm 29 April](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/2IWgCr814vI869GOYSysnf4Gt4E?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌") 

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌") 

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below: 

‌

**Role** 

**Name** 

**Contact Details** 

 **Support Provided** 

 **Time Allocation** 

**Available Timeframe** 

**Curriculum & Education Developer** 

‌

**(CED)** 

Chris Della Vedova 

[Chris.DellaVedova@unisa.edu.au](mailto:Chris.DellaVedova@unisa.edu.au "‌") 

Provides curriculum and pedagogical advice and support including course planning, assessments, course overview, and high-level module/topic structure.  

5 hours 

‌

**Weeks 1 to 4:** 

Monday 21 April – 16 May 

‌

**Learning Designer** 

‌

**(LD)** 

Richard Bartlett 

[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌") 

Contributes to the identification and design of media and learning activities (for face-to-face and online formats). They may also help to extend the course plan. 

15 hours 

**Weeks 4 to 8:** 

‌

Monday 12 May – Friday 13 June 

‌

\*Please note, both your CED & LD are available during week 4 

**Course Build Support** 

Your Learning Designer will arrange course build support. 

Builds some content in the LMS; completion of fundamental ad-hoc design tasks. 

‌

20 hours 

**Weeks 6 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 26 May – Friday 27 June 

‌

‌

**Media Design** 

\-Email [mediaproduction@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:mediaproduction@adelaide.edu.au "‌") to schedule your consult 

 
\-More information [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-booking-consultations-and-workshops#mediaContent "‌") and a useful [help guide](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/designing-your-media "‌") 

Provides guidance on selecting the best types of media for courses and assists with preparing for the development of a range of media types. 

5 hours 

‌

**Weeks 4 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 12 May – Friday 27 June 

**Media Production** 

Your consult with Media Design will guide any next steps 

Supports developers to produce media content (through their team of skilled multimedia designers, professional recording studios, and self-serve facilities and equipment). 

15 hours 

**Weeks 6 to 10:** 

‌

Monday 26 May – Friday 27 June 

‌

(course readings and resource selection) 

[library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌") 

Support in identifying resources that align with your course. Copyright guidance. 

‌

As needed 

**Recommended in Weeks 2 to 4:** 

‌

Monday 28 April – Friday 16 May 

**Academic Transition Panel**  

**(ATP)** 

Andrew MacKinnon (UoA), Emma Sherratt (UoA), Tom Raimondo (UniSA), & Craig Williams (UniSA) 

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development. 

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns. 

‌

‌

**Curriculum Quality Panel**   

**(CQP)**  

Curriculum Quality Panel members for each discipline oversee and lead the course quality review process and are available to provide pedagogical and discipline-specific guidance during course development. They will be invited to attend milestone meetings and will introduce themselves then. 

‌

‌

You can continue with the design work you have been doing. I look forward to seeing you at our first meeting next week. You may wish to contact your CED to organise a time asap next week.  

‌

Kind regards, 

**Sophia Carson**  

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

old.desc:
Hi Kezia,

Thanks again for the updates—really helpful to get this visibility. I’d be happy to join the Georesources team meeting this Friday at 9:30am, so please do go ahead and add me in.

‌

For **Bioinformatics: Sequencing Technologies**, I’ve had a look at the Canvas shell and noticed it’s currently structured around four topic-style blocks rather than distinct modules:

- Fundamentals of Bioinformatics
- Genomics
- Transcriptomics
- Evolutionary Genomics (Optional)
  ‌

Would you be able to bring me up to speed on the thinking behind this approach? I wasn’t sure if the intent was to keep things stacked and thematic rather than following a modular progression.

In comparison, Bioinformatics: Overview has three defined modules—

1. **Fundamentals** of **Bioinformatics**,
2. **Pathogens and Metagenomics,** and
3. **Reproducible Science**
   —and ten topics across them.

‌

Just wondering if there might be potential to adapt some of that material for use in Sequencing Technologies, particularly from Fundamentals and Reproducible Science, given the alignment between the two courses.

Sophia also noted the following items as outstanding. Let me know if you’re across any of these or just waiting for the academics to do their part?

- Course Details Form
- Course Alignment Map
- Assessment Rubric
- Course Content Breakdown
  ‌

For **Georesources Industry Project**, I’ve looked at what’s in development within the Canvas Modules and can see two clear clusters—Research Report and Research Communication—with a number of draft and published pages under each. It looks like a solid starting point.

That said, I was looking at the “How to write your thesis” page in particular, and noticed it currently includes just two PDF links with what’s likely some really valuable content inside. Do you think there might be appetite from Monica, Simon or Stijn to unpack some of that material directly on the page, using the PDFs as supplementary resources rather than the main content?

From my end, I’m happy to offer some light-touch suggestions for making these pages more student-facing—things like:

- Structuring pages to align more clearly with assessment touchpoints (e.g. proposal → thesis → presentation)
- Clarifying what content is essential vs supplementary
- Including some brief scaffolding or reflection prompts to guide students through their self-directed learning
  ‌

That said, I’ve not worked extensively on MSc-level courses before, so I’m more than happy to take the lead from you and the course builders.

Let me know what you think and if there’s anything you’d like me to prepare ahead of Friday.

‌

Cheers,

‌

**Rich Bartlett**

card.desc:
**Where it’s up to:**

- Canvas shell includes early content grouped under two key themes:
  - _Research Report_ (e.g. scientific writing, literature review, editing)
  - _Research Communication_ (e.g. oral presentation preparation)
- Pages vary in status—some are published, some remain unpublished.
- Much of the page content currently consists of links to PDFs.

**Complexities:**

- MSc-level self-directed research course with no lectures or scheduled teaching.
- Runs parallel to an Honours research course (previously developed).
- Assessment structure:
  - Thesis (60%)
  - Research proposal (20%)
  - Poster presentation (20%)
- Emphasis on soft skills (writing, presenting), with minimal structured teaching.

**LD contributions (initial suggestions):**

- Assist in lightly framing and structuring content to align with assessment milestones.
- Offer suggestions on unpacking PDF content into more accessible Canvas pages.
- Suggest minimal scaffolding or pacing prompts to guide students through self-directed work.
- Help ensure student-facing pages are clear, consistent, and supportive of independent learning.

Learning Designer FHEA

**Online Programs Team | Learning Enhancement & Innovation**
Division of Academic and Student Engagement

**THE UNIVERSITY OF ADELAIDE**
Room 661, Level 6, Kenneth Wills Building
T: +61 0467 982 427 
E: [rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌")
