‌

‌

**From:** Kezia Perry <[Kezia.Perry@unisa.edu.au](mailto:Kezia.Perry@unisa.edu.au "‌")\>
**Date:** Thursday, 22 May 2025 at 9:23 am
**To:** Rich Bartlett <[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>
**Subject:** RE: Week 5 Handover Cycle 8 updates

Hi Rich,

‌

I thought I would give you updates on these two subjects and their progress. I have a meeting with the Georesources team this Friday at 9.30 if you are able to join us, I can add you the meeting. 

‌

‌

**Bioinformatics: Sequencing Technologies**

‌

This course started development last November and they had an outdated course rubric but not much else. They’ve had a lost of absence due to illness and leave. They have 3 modules based on current courses, but it needs rework. The main issue in this course is that a fundamental module is being taught across two courses so there is overlap. The module being shared is called Fundamentals of Bioinformatics and it was developed in Cycle 2 Course code is [Bioinformatics Overview](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/Science/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2Faupcd%2FScience%2FBioinformatics%20%2D%20Overview%5FPHAR%5F5002&viewid=4b6f4a2a%2Ddbcc%2D46a1%2D9a03%2Ddb12cf5b7265 "‌"). The CD’s don’t know if they should teach the same content- but give different assessment. Ideal scenario is students do either course. There are no lectures in this course only face to face practicals. So, they want to treat the modules like they are stacked. The courses run in different semesters so its all complicated.

‌

The PO for this subject Sophia had really limited help for us in this area.  I’m checking with Sarah Davey from our program group about some of this.

‌

‌

‌

**Georesources Industry Project**

‌

Another complex subject. I was talking to Sarah and Tim about this one on the program group the other day.  This is a self directed study course- research project. No talk component, no lecture component. MSC research project. Runs parallel with honours research project which has already been developed in cycle 2 by Corinne Green. Emphasis on soft skills like writing a thesis, presenting results. Assessment will be Thesis 60% Research proposal 20% and poster presentation 20%.

‌

‌

If you have any questions or want to attend the Friday meeting, please let me know.

‌

Kind regards,

Kezia

‌

‌

**Dr Kezia Perry. PhD, MA., BA.,**

Lecturer: Academic Development | Teaching Innovation Unit

‌

Kaurna Country

David Pank Building | City West Campus

University of South Australia

[kezia.perry@unisa.edu.au](mailto:kezia.perry@unisa.edu.au "‌") | p +61 8 8302 5478 |

‌

CRICOS Provider No. 00121B

‌

‌

The University of Adelaide and the University of South Australia are

combining their strengths to create a new university for the future.

card.desc:
**Where it’s up to:**

- Canvas shell includes four topic-style blocks:
  - _Fundamentals of Bioinformatics_
  - _Genomics_
  - _Transcriptomics_
  - _Evolutionary Genomics (Optional)_
- Some content may be adapted from _Bioinformatics: Overview_, which includes three modules and 10 topics.
- Limited progress on documentation (e.g. Course Details Form, Alignment Map, Assessment Rubric, Content Breakdown).

**Complexities:**

- Content overlaps with another course (_Bioinformatics Overview_), particularly around the _Fundamentals_ module.
- The courses run in different semesters and share practical components but have different assessments.
- No lectures—only face-to-face practicals. The team is considering a "stacked" delivery model.

**LD contributions (initial suggestions):**

- Support with mapping shared vs unique content between courses, ensuring clarity and coherence.
- Light assistance in reworking existing content to suit a stacked/module-lite delivery.
- Help structure or format content for Canvas based on the academic team’s direction.
- Assist with outstanding documentation if guidance is provided.

Do you think there might be appetite from Monica, Simon or Stijn to unpack some of that material directly on the page, using the PDFs as supplementary resources rather than the main content?

From my end, I’m happy to offer some light-touch suggestions for making these pages more student-facing—things like:

- Structuring pages to align more clearly with assessment touchpoints (e.g. proposal → thesis → presentation)
- Clarifying what content is essential vs supplementary
- Including some brief scaffolding or reflection prompts to guide students through their self-directed learning
  ‌

That said, I’ve not worked extensively on MSc-level courses before, so I’m more than happy to take the lead from you and the course builders.

Let me know what you think and if there’s anything you’d like me to prepare ahead of Friday.

‌

Cheers,

‌

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

**Online Programs Team | Learning Enhancement & Innovation**
Division of Academic and Student Engagement

**To:** Rich Bartlett <[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>
**Subject:** RE: Week 5 Handover Cycle 8 updates

Hi Rich,

‌

I thought I would give you updates on these two subjects and their progress. I have a meeting with the Georesources team this Friday at 9.30 if you are able to join us, I can add you the meeting. 

‌

‌

**Bioinformatics: Sequencing Technologies**

‌

This course started development last November and they had an outdated course rubric but not much else. They’ve had a lost of absence due to illness and leave. They have 3 modules based on current courses, but it needs rework. The main issue in this course is that a fundamental module is being taught across two courses so there is overlap. The module being shared is called Fundamentals of Bioinformatics and it was developed in Cycle 2 Course code is [Bioinformatics Overview](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/Science/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2Faupcd%2FScience%2FBioinformatics%20%2D%20Overview%5FPHAR%5F5002&viewid=4b6f4a2a%2Ddbcc%2D46a1%2D9a03%2Ddb12cf5b7265 "‌"). The CD’s don’t know if they should teach the same content- but give different assessment. Ideal scenario is students do either course. There are no lectures in this course only face to face practicals. So, they want to treat the modules like they are stacked. The courses run in different semesters so its all complicated.

‌

The PO for this subject Sophia had really limited help for us in this area.  I’m checking with Sarah Davey from our program group about some of this.

‌

‌

‌

**Georesources Industry Project**

‌

Another complex subject. I was talking to Sarah and Tim about this one on the program group the other day.  This is a self directed study course- research project. No talk component, no lecture component. MSC research project. Runs parallel with honours research project which has already been developed in cycle 2 by Corinne Green. Emphasis on soft skills like writing a thesis, presenting results. Assessment will be Thesis 60% Research proposal 20% and poster presentation 20%.

‌

‌

If you have any questions or want to attend the Friday meeting, please let me know.

‌

Kind regards,

Kezia

data.text:
Hi Alexander, Juraj & Monica,

Thanks again for the meeting today

‌

Just following up on a couple of points we discussed:

\@Sarah – Would you be able to confirm if the genetics eLab book you mentioned to the team previously can be used as an online workbook for this course? As you know, the plan is for students to record their weekly practical outcomes directly into the eLab book, which would then be assessed at the end of the course. **Is this a bespoke tool for Moodle/UniSA online that could be adapted?** If this isn’t possible, we’re exploring Cadmus as a backup. 

‌

\@Jack (our Digital Education Designer) can investigate Cadmus further and connect with the Capability Development Team to identify any known usability issues. He could also assist in identifying a student-facing video guide if needed.

---

**Course structure request**

To help us get started on the Canvas build, could Alexander, Juraj & Monica please revise your Excel document so that each topic clearly follows this format:

‌

**[Module title]: [Topic title]**

This naming will ensure clarity across all ten topics (representing ten weeks of learning). As you have three modules, they will repeat across different topics. Each topic should also list the concept pages you'd like included (equivalent to the individual content pages as shown in the attached example). Don’t worry – We can have some flexibility in the naming of things that will help the students understand their navigation through the course.

‌

To guide this, here’s an example prototype using your existing content:

(Module 1)
Elements on Earth: solid rocks to the oceans

Topic 1 overview

**KEY CONCEPTS**

- Introduction to elemental formation
- Nuclear synthesis and stellar origins
- Rock specimen analysis using Tornado data
- Interbedded technique videos (e.g. XRF, XRD)

**KEY EVENTS**

- Prac 1 – Solid Earth Geochemistry

Topic 1 Summary

‌

---

‌

(Module 1 again)

Elements on Earth: High vs low temperature environments

Topic 2 overview

**KEY CONCEPTS**

- Elemental analysis techniques (XRF, ICP-OES, CRDS)
- Concluding observations

**KEY EVENT**

- Prac 1 Conclusion

Topic 2 summary

Would you be happy to format your existing spreadsheet similarly on a new tab, perhaps or feel free to translate into a word doc, whatever is easier? This will give us a solid foundation to begin the Canvas structure aligned with topic flow and assessment design.

‌

Kind regards,

Rich Bartlett

Learning Designer FHEA

card.desc:
#### **Summary of work completed by LD (beyond LD remit)**

To support project continuity and maintain development momentum by week 4 of the 10-week cycle, the following tasks have been undertaken by me as LD, which would typically sit within the CED’s scope:

- **Curriculum mapping**
  Developed a full draft of the course map, structured into 3 curriculum modules and 10 topics, aligned to the course aim and CLOs.
- **Topic conceptualisation**
  Outlined key concepts for each topic, mapped to Canvas page structure (i.e. child pages), to ensure ease of content delivery planning and site build.
- **Assessment summary**
  Drafted a scaffolded, high-level assessment structure including task types, timing, CLO alignment, and estimated weightings, based on existing planning documents and informed by AI support.
- **Communication support**
  Drafted a detailed summary email to Course Developers explaining the intent and structure of the draft materials, while managing expectations around fidelity and constructive alignment.
- **Stakeholder coordination**
  Confirmed with Sarah (CED) that she will step in during Chris’s leave and help facilitate the next meeting, ensuring handover continuity and learner experience considerations are maintained.

---

#### **Outstanding work not yet completed by CD(s)**

As of week 4, the following tasks remain incomplete or require input/ownership from the Course Developers:

- **Confirmation or revision of CLO alignment**
  CLO-to-assessment and CLO-to-activity mapping has not been finalised by the CD(s), and feedback is required on whether the current CLOs adequately reflect intended learning and skill development.
- **Approval of course structure and sequencing**
  The proposed module/topic structure is yet to be confirmed or revised by the CD(s).
- **Detailed learning design per topic**
  Content development at the topic/concept level (e.g. readings, videos, quiz items, lab guides) is still outstanding.
- **Assessment rubric development**
  While partially outlined in existing documents, not all assessments have complete or finalised rubrics linked to CLOs or learning outcomes.
- **Aboriginal Knowledges integration**
  No direction has yet been provided on how Indigenous perspectives or content will be incorporated into the course.

data.text:
Hi Jane,

Following up on our meeting, I’ve gone ahead and created an initial structure within your AU Canvas course space to support further development of the assessments and content.

You’ll see I’ve set up four assignment groups, based on my interpretation of what was discussed in our meeting with Chris. These are just placeholders for now and can absolutely be changed or edited as needed to better reflect the final structure. You can view them here:

👉 [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2690/assignments](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2690/assignments "‌")

‌

‌

‌

I’ve also added a template page within the course that you can use to fill in the details for each specific assessment task. As mentioned, rubrics for the Poster and Group Presentation can be extracted directly from the Course Details Form once you’ve completed those sections.

‌

As a next step, it would be helpful to have an outline document that maps out your topics across the 10-week structure, aligned with the AU curriculum model. Something like the below format would be ideal:

‌

[Module Name]: Topic #1 – [Topic title] 

→ Key Concepts: [List of key concepts covered in this topic] 

→ Key Events: [Workshop, discussion, quiz, or other learning events for this topic]

‌

Once we have this structure you can then start copying content from our UoA course and I can help uplift these pages when back from leave on the 28th April

Let me know if you have any questions or if you'd like to work through this together. Happy to jump on a teams call if that's easier.

‌

Best,

‌

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

old.desc:
2 HRS

This revised H5P drag and drop activity represents a focused uplift to improve clarity, consistency, and alignment with learning outcomes. The redesign involved the following key improvements:

- **Instructional clarity:** Clearer directions and the addition of a scenario help frame the task within a workplace context, reinforcing relevance and encouraging applied thinking.
- **Refined phrasing of items:** All draggable items were reviewed and revised to reflect clear, actionable tasks rather than abstract labels. This enhances learner understanding and reduces ambiguity, supporting more confident decision-making during the activity.
- **Terminology consistency:** While an initial version used action-oriented category phrasing (e.g. _Control applications_), these were later reverted to established terms (e.g. _Application Control_) at the request of the Course Developer to ensure alignment with recognised cybersecurity terminology. This decision reflects a balance between instructional consistency and discipline-specific accuracy.
- **Scenario-based context:** The inclusion of a concise scenario enhances the authenticity of the task, encouraging learners to think critically about how different controls are applied in real-world systems administration.
- **Improved future adaptability:** The revised structure allows for more accessible formatting and easier integration of adaptive feedback. It also positions the activity for potential reuse or modification in future iterations of the course.

This uplift has strengthened the pedagogical integrity of the activity while respecting discipline conventions, resulting in a more purposeful and learner-friendly experience.

card.desc:
2 HRS

This revised H5P drag and drop activity represents a targeted uplift focused on clarity, consistency, and instructional integrity. The enhancements are designed to align more closely with learning outcomes while supporting an effective and fair learner experience.

- **Instructional clarity:** Updated directions and the addition of a workplace-based scenario help frame the task with practical relevance, encouraging applied thinking.
- **Refined phrasing of items:** Draggable items were reworded for consistency, ensuring each describes a clear, actionable task. This reduces ambiguity and supports more confident engagement with the content.
- **Terminology accuracy:** While early drafts used action-oriented phrasing for categories, these were later revised to retain standard cybersecurity terms (e.g. _Application Control_) based on academic guidance. This change ensures alignment with established discipline language.
- **Scenario-based context:** A concise, authentic scenario positions the activity in a workplace setting, supporting the application of theory to practice.
- **Improved interaction integrity:** All draggable items are now allowed to be dropped into any category box (rather than only their correct one), and answers are only validated on submission. This prevents students from using trial-and-error during the activity, preserving the integrity of the task as a formative learning opportunity.
- **Future adaptability and accessibility:** The structure of the revised activity lends itself to future enhancements, such as adaptive feedback, screen reader support, or scenario variations.

This uplift reflects a balance between pedagogical consistency, discipline-specific accuracy, and user experience, resulting in a stronger and more authentic learning activity.

data.text:
Quick win to share—I've uplifted a H5P flashcard activity in a Security Foundations course that was just being used like a slideshow. It's now a proper formative learning tool where students actively test their understanding of the ACS ethical values. I also reworked the surrounding text so the answers are clearly embedded in the content, making it easier for students to connect the dots and build confidence. A small shift, but a solid uplift for the students and academic.

‌

Perhaps another example of doing some course build work and Learning Design as HE07 and not breaking the bank or budget in terms of time allocation. Putting that task into [http://Monday.com](http://Monday.com "smartCard-inline")  and liaising (positively) with a CCB would have taken the same amount of time for me to rework the H5P.

card.desc:
45 MINS

**Flashcard uplift – ethics in cybersecurity (ACS Code of Conduct)**

**Overview of changes:**

- Transformed a passive image-based H5P flashcard activity into an interactive, formative learning tool.
- Reworded the page content to **embed the exact answers used in the flashcards** within the explanatory text.
- Ensured alignment with the **Australian Computer Society (ACS) Code of Professional Conduct (2014)** using source language to maintain authenticity and clarity.
- Refined the structure of each card to include:
  - A **targeted question**
  - A **shortened, flexible answer** using keywords
  - A **tip** to prompt recall without revealing the answer

**Benefits for students:**

- Supports **active recall** rather than passive reading
- Reinforces key ethical concepts in a low-stakes, **self-check format**
- Embedding answer phrases directly in the content scaffolds learning and **improves accessibility**
- Builds confidence through **formative feedback** and guidance without relying on assessment pressure
- Clearer structure encourages **independent engagement** with professional values

**Benefits for teaching team:**

- Demonstrates alignment with ACS professional standards
- Encourages students to develop foundational understanding of professional ethics early
- Easy to adapt or replicate across other topics requiring applied ethical reasoning

data.text:
Hi Rich,

‌

I'm sending my apology because i met with Ross today and had included a [Rich.bartlett@unisa.edu.au](mailto:Rich.bartlett@unisa.edu.au "‌") to the teams link and not yourself with the UoAdelaide email address. I had not picked up on this at the time.

‌

We spent the meeting discussing various changes since Ross's last course in cycle 3.

‌

Main heads up so far is that Ross intends to keep a lot of the original course and adjust it to the 10 weeks however he's previously delivered 3 hr face to face lectures each week and a 1 hour workshop so may need some support re-imaging how to deliver the content. I mentioned it doesn't need to be just clipping those lectures into 15 min blocks but instead exploring other ways for students to access content such as readings, interactives etc.

‌

Current assessment discussion has been to look at instead of weekly workshop question submissions (more or less an attendance or participation mark) doing a portfolio submission of problems solved by the end of the year with supporting formative weekly questions to practice.

‌

Other assessments discussed were 1 test and a final exam. I didn't spend long on the assessment discussion because i wanted to review the CLOs and draft the rubric first to help inform the assessment plan. 

‌

That's all for now, we plan to meet next monday again if you're free, teams meeting request should've come through this time.

‌

Kind regards,

Sarah

‌

**Dr Sarah Davey** | Lecturer: Academic Developer

University of South Australia: Teaching Innovation Unit

CS5-10 City West Campus 

Phone:  8302 2390

E-mail: [s](mailto:evonne.macmaster@unisa.edu.au "‌")[arah.davey@unisa.edu.au](mailto:arah.davey@unisa.edu.au "‌")

‌

[www.unisa.edu.au](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/NczKCp815qInQJMNYSDhpUG7jd9?domain=unisa.edu.au/ "‌") |

card.desc:
Dear Ross, 

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **Classical Physics 2** course, scheduled for development from **17 March 2025** to **23 May 2025**. 

‌

My name is Rebecca McLachlan and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below).  

‌

**Course kick-off** 

Week 1: w/c 17 March 2025 

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design 

Week 4: w/c 7 April 2025 

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development 

Week 7: w/c 28 April 2025 

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development Completion 

Week 10: w/c 19 May 2025 

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_ 

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link**.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2638 "‌") 

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins. 

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌") 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌") 

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌") 

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Book an appointment [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-and-booking-consultations "‌") 

‌

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

[10am –12pm 13 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/S893Clx14mu21JV5KuGf5Uz7lur?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌") 

‌

[1 – 3pm 14 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/mmz-CmO54nijpwgKYUOh1URH4je?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌")  

‌

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌") 

‌

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below: 

‌

**Role** 

**Name** 

**Email** 

 **Support Provided** 

 **Time Allocation** 

**Available Timeframe** 

**Curriculum & Education Developer** 

‌

**(CED)** 

Sarah Davey 

[sarah.davey@unisa.edu.au](mailto:sarah.davey@unisa.edu.au "‌") 

Provides curriculum and pedagogical advice and support including course planning, assessments, course overview, and high-level module/topic structure.  

5 hours 

‌

**Weeks 2 to 5:** 

‌

Monday 24 March – Friday 18 April 

‌

**Learning Designer** 

‌

**(LD)** 

Richard Bartlett 

[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌") 

Contributes to the identification and design of media and learning activities (for face-to-face and online formats). They may also help to extend the course plan. 

15 hours 

**Weeks 4 to 8:** 

‌

Monday 7 April – Friday 9 May 

‌

\*Please note, both your CED & LD are available during week 4

Hi Sunny and Paula,

Just FYI on where I'm at with the course, which may guide our discussion this afternoon.

- Media team are rebranding the PDFs on each concept page, removing the course code and also converting to PPTX for future requirements
- I've swept the whole course and converted any formative quizzes into H5P quizzes and embedded them on the page
- I've added all academic references at the end of each Topic Summary page

There's still a bunch of missing readings/H5P activities which I can't identify nor have access to some Moddle, I've \@ tagged sunny on the relevant pages in canvas so when Sunny does a sweet hopefully these can be added.

Considering the time left (this week and next) it might be worth Sunny telling me what readings are missing and I can add these to the course readings list and then embed on the page as this can take a bit of admin time.

Cheers,

card.name: Emailed Sally as a courtesy follow up

data.text:
Hi Sally,

‌

I hope you're doing well. I’m Rich, the LD assigned to your AU course development. I wanted to check in on the development of From Bones to Holograms: Laboratory Techniques for Archaeology. It’s great to see the Canvas site taking shape, with three modules now populated with key activities like lectures and practicals. The content is looking strong, and I can see the effort that’s gone into structuring it.

‌

One thing to flag is that aligning the course structure more closely with the conventions outlined in the Course Development Essentials site will help ensure a smoother QA process with the IMO project officer.

‌

The key difference is that at present it seems the current structure is more theme-based, focusing on curriculum naming, whereas the recommended approach is to organise content into **10** x Curriculum Module names + clear Topic names, each containing structured pages for overviews, key concepts, activities, and summaries.

‌

To help visualise what this could look like, I’ve attached a PDF screenshot from another course that follows the recommended structure—just as an example of the ideal format. Of course, your content and approach may differ, but using this structure will help streamline navigation for students and meet QA expectations.

‌

Other suggested changes might include reworking your Lecture pages _into_ “Concept Pages” as from what I can see the content on these pages seems to align more with what is being defined as a Key Concept.

‌

Anyway, I don’t mean to be a burden on your time or this process, just letting you know I’d be happy to chat through this in more detail if you’d find it helpful. Let me know what you think and we can schedule a time to chat early next week whilst we still have time in Dev cycle 5.

‌

Best,

‌

**Rich Bartlett**

card.desc:
Dear Amanda, 

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **Ethics and Professionalism (Financial Planning)** course, scheduled for development from **10th of February to 18th of April 2025**.

‌

My name is Kate and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below). 

‌

**Course kick-off**

Week 1: w/c **10th of February 2025**

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design

Week 4: w/c **3rd of March 2025**

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development

Week 7: w/c **24th of March 2025**

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development Completion

Week 10: w/c **14th of April 2025**

‌

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources**

Resource

Description

Link

**Course Shell Link**

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content.

[Course Shell Link.](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2593 "‌")

**Course Development Journey**

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins.

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌")

**AU Course Development Essentials Site**

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete. 

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌")

**SharePoint Folder**

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work.

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name)

‌

**Training & Support**

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources.

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌")

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool. 

Book an appointment [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-and-booking-consultations "‌")

‌

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments.

[10am –12pm 13 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/dO3uC5QPGriZZxQxXHzf2TkbtEi?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌")

‌

[1 – 3pm 14 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/D4g0C6XQJvFrr1A1pt6hwT5WnHe?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌")

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building.

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌")

‌

**Key Contacts:**

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below:

‌

**Name**

**Email**

**Support Provided**

**Time Allocation**

**Available Timeframe**

‌

**(CED)**

Sasikala Rathnappulige

[sasikala.rathnappulige@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:sasikala.rathnappulige@adelaide.edu.au "‌")

5 hours

20 hours

‌

As needed

17th Feb - 7th March

**(ATP)**

Lin Crase (UniSA) & Alfred Yawson  (UoA)

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns. 

‌

‌

‌

**Next Steps:** 

Next week is the first week of development and I encourage you to continue exploring the [Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌") with **Week 1: Onboarding.** 

‌

I look forward to seeing you at our kick-off meeting. 

‌

Kind regards,

‌

**Kate Bacon**

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

‌

**Integration Management Office** 

Level 4/5, 108 North Terrace, Adelaide SA 5000
**P** (08) 8302 0320
**E:** [kate.bacon@unisa.edu.au](mailto:kate.bacon@unisa.edu.au "‌")

[_**Creating a university for the future**_](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/mUWNC71RKwiAAQxQ8uBiRTotpoy?domain=adelaideuni.edu.au "‌")

card.desc:
Dear Daniel, Winston and Xiaohui, 

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **Business Valuation** course, scheduled for development from **10th of February to 18th of April 2025**.

‌

My name is Kate and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below). 

‌

**Course kick-off**

Week 1: w/c **10th of February 2025**

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design

Week 4: w/c **3rd of March 2025**

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development

Week 7: w/c **24th of March 2025**

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development Completion

Week 10: w/c **14th of April 2025**

‌

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources**

Resource

Description

Link

**Course Shell Link**

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content.

[Course Shell Link.](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2592 "‌")

**Course Development Journey**

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins.

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌")

**AU Course Development Essentials Site**

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete. 

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌")

**SharePoint Folder**

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work.

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name)

‌

**Training & Support**

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources.

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌")

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool. 

Book an appointment [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-and-booking-consultations "‌")

‌

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments.

[10am –12pm 13 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/Pt9jC4QO0qiBB9wY5HOf9T46QAc?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌")

‌

[1 – 3pm 14 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/tR9HC5QPGriZZprg9fOh2TkJdqj?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌")

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building.

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌")

‌

**Key Contacts:**

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below:

card.name: Rebranded videos - link

card.desc:
Hi Georgina,

‌

Thanks for taking the time to review the prototype and for your feedback. I completely understand your concerns about the branching simulation not fully capturing the nuance needed for a social science analysis—happy to drop that idea and pivot to other interactive approaches if/as required.

‌

Great to hear that you’re nearly done with organising the weekly readings. Just checking, I noticed you have the following reading on the Topic 1 [concept page](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2114/pages/cultural-ecology-and-cultural-landscapes "‌"), however, it doesn’t seem to appear in the Course Readings List.

_Baer, Hans. A. 2008. “Toward a Critical Anthropology on the Impact of Global Warming on Health and Human Societies.” Medical Anthropology 27(1): 2-8. _[_https://doi.org/10.1080/01459740701831369Links_](https://doi.org/10.1080/01459740701831369Links "‌")_ to an external site._

‌

‌

![Image](cid:1014873164*image005.png@01DB788B.CC473E90)

‌

You may also noticed I’ve “wrapped” this reading in a blue direction box. This is a standard practice we’re using as part of the new AU style guide. Are you happy for me to do this for all your readings on relevant pages?

‌

‌

‌

Once Jaye is back and working on the video- and reading-based quizzes, I’ll be happy to assist as needed in Weeks 7-8 while I’m still available. Since she’ll be handling the reading summaries and PowerPoints, that sounds like a good way to ensure key concepts are reinforced in the quizzes.

![Image](cid:1014873164*image006.png@01DB788B.E74D1F20)

‌

It’s also great to hear that you’ve got the videos in place for each topic and the asynchronous pages. 

‌

For the interactive seminar pages, would you like me to wrap the wording in an “activity box” similar to the reading box above? In the same task, I’m happy to help with stepping out instructions and implementing additional structure so that all students benefit from the guidance you undoubtedly provide. This will ensure all students are on the same page and there’s consistency throughout the course pages.

I can simply provide structure to the text with relevant headings, etc, and then you’re more than welcome to edit/tweak as you see fit. 

‌

Please see this page> [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2114/pages/interactive-seminar?module\_item\_id=78330](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2114/pages/interactive-seminar?module_item_id=78330 "‌")

cards.name: Applied Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning

cards.desc:
This course started development last November and they had an outdated course rubric but not much else. They’ve had a lost of absence due to illness and leave. They have 3 modules based on current courses, but it needs rework. The main issue in this course is that a fundamental module is being taught across two courses so there is overlap. The module being shared is called Fundamentals of Bioinformatics and it was developed in Cycle 2 Course code is [Bioinformatics Overview](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/Science/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2Faupcd%2FScience%2FBioinformatics%20%2D%20Overview%5FPHAR%5F5002&viewid=4b6f4a2a%2Ddbcc%2D46a1%2D9a03%2Ddb12cf5b7265 "‌"). The CD’s don’t know if they should teach the same content- but give different assessment. Ideal scenario is students do either course. There are no lectures in this course only face to face practicals. So, they want to treat the modules like they are stacked. The courses run in different semesters so its all complicated. 

‌

The PO for this subject Sophia had really limited help for us in this area.  I’m checking with Sarah Davey from our program group about some of this.
cards.name: Overview

cards.desc:
**From:** Sophia Carson <[sophia.carson@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:sophia.carson@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>
**Date:** Tuesday, 20 May 2025 at 2:08 pm
**To:** Anna Sheppard <[anna.sheppard@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:anna.sheppard@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>, James Botten <[james.botten@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:james.botten@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>, Christopher Wong <[christopher.wong@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:christopher.wong@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>
**Cc:** Jessica Webb <[jessica.webb@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:jessica.webb@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>, Katherine Pillman <[katherine.pillman@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:katherine.pillman@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>, Rich Bartlett <[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌")\>
**Subject:** Milestone 2 meeting - Follow up

Hello Team,

‌

Following on from our Milestone Meeting on 12 May, I reviewed the Course Details Form and the Canvas course shell. Below are the key items that still require attention:

‌

**Course Details Form**

Course Details Form – pending completion

Course Alignment Map

Assessment Rubric

Course Content Breakdown – currently only one module completed

‌

**Canvas Shell – Pending Items**

Welcome Message & Introductory Video

Assignments & Rubrics – to be built in Canvas

Course Readings – if any, these need to be referenced in the content and linked via Leganto

Discussion Boards – if included, the Learning Designer can assist with setup

Quizzes – to be created

Modules & Topics – to be populated in Canvas

Course Conclusion – to be developed

If any content is to be carried over from an existing course, please coordinate with our Learning Designer, Richard Bartlett, as this will also require rebranding of materials (including videos).

‌

‌

Our next milestone meeting is scheduled for **2 June**, and I hope that some of the above items can be completed by then.

‌

‌

Kind regards,

**Sophia Carson**  

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum
cards.name: Email from PO - Sophia

cards.desc:
**Where it’s up to:**

- Canvas shell includes four topic-style blocks:
  - _Fundamentals of Bioinformatics_
  - _Genomics_
  - _Transcriptomics_
  - _Evolutionary Genomics (Optional)_
- Some content may be adapted from _Bioinformatics: Overview_, which includes three modules and 10 topics.
- Limited progress on documentation (e.g. Course Details Form, Alignment Map, Assessment Rubric, Content Breakdown).

**Complexities:**

- Content overlaps with another course (_Bioinformatics Overview_), particularly around the _Fundamentals_ module.
- The courses run in different semesters and share practical components but have different assessments.
- No lectures—only face-to-face practicals. The team is considering a "stacked" delivery model.

**LD contributions (initial suggestions):**

- Support with mapping shared vs unique content between courses, ensuring clarity and coherence.
- Light assistance in reworking existing content to suit a stacked/module-lite delivery.
- Help structure or format content for Canvas based on the academic team’s direction.
- Assist with outstanding documentation if guidance is provided.

Hi Anna,

Thanks for looping me in.

I’ve now duplicated the modules from Bioinformatics: Overview _**into**_ the Bioinformatics: Sequencing Technologies Canvas shell, so things are mirrored as requested.

‌

Please let me know if there’s anything else I can assist with at this stage.

‌

Just a quick note — I’ll be away on leave from Friday 30 May to Thursday 5 June, but I’ll do my best to wrap up anything outstanding before then. Ofcourse we can catch up on my return, we’ll then be at the end of Cycle 7 with three more weeks to go.

‌

Kindly,

‌

cards.name: No engagement - Emailed PO

cards.desc: Another complex subject. I was talking to Sarah and Tim about this one on the program group the other day.  This is a self directed study course- research project. No talk component, no lecture component. MSC research project. Runs parallel with honours research project which has already been developed in cycle 2 by Corinne Green. Emphasis on soft skills like writing a thesis, presenting results. Assessment will be Thesis 60% Research proposal 20% and poster presentation 20%.
cards.name: Overview

cards.desc:
**Where it’s up to:**

- Canvas shell includes early content grouped under two key themes:
  - _Research Report_ (e.g. scientific writing, literature review, editing)
  - _Research Communication_ (e.g. oral presentation preparation)
- Pages vary in status—some are published, some remain unpublished.
- Much of the page content currently consists of links to PDFs.

**Complexities:**

- MSc-level self-directed research course with no lectures or scheduled teaching.
- Runs parallel to an Honours research course (previously developed).
- Assessment structure:
  - Thesis (60%)
  - Research proposal (20%)
  - Poster presentation (20%)
- Emphasis on soft skills (writing, presenting), with minimal structured teaching.

**LD contributions (initial suggestions):**

- Assist in lightly framing and structuring content to align with assessment milestones.
- Offer suggestions on unpacking PDF content into more accessible Canvas pages.
- Suggest minimal scaffolding or pacing prompts to guide students through self-directed work.
- Help ensure student-facing pages are clear, consistent, and supportive of independent learning.
cards.name: Current state of course 22/5

cards.desc:
Hi Monica,

Thanks again for your detailed feedback and context around the Canvas structure and timeline image. Based on your notes, here’s the draft email I’m planning to send to the media team (along with Simon’s image) to kick off the request on your behalf.

Could you please take a moment to review and let me know if it captures everything correctly or if anything needs adjustment before I submit the formal ticket?

---

_**Draft email to media team:**_

I’m reaching out regarding the creation of a timeline image for the _Georesources Industry Project_ course (MSc level), based on direction and feedback from the academic team (Monica, Simon, and Stijn).

I’ve attached a **hand-drawn reference image from Simon (Course Builder)** to help guide the general layout and structure. Please note, some of the information in the sketch is no longer current—so please refer to the notes below for accuracy.

### Key instructions and updates:

- **Updated timeframe**
  The course runs from **July to November** (Semester 2). The original sketch shows February to November—please adjust the timeline to reflect this updated semester span.
- **Consistent naming**
  The timeline should use **the same titles as the Canvas module groups**:
  - _Research Proposal_
  - _Research Report_
  - _Research Communication_
    These align with the “activity waves” shown in the hand-drawn version, so please match the wording accordingly for student clarity.
- **Visual structure**
  The academic team would like the image to include the following elements:
  - **A horizontal timeline** from July to November with reference points around mid-August, September, October, and November
  - **Three overlapping phases**, shown as stylised curves or waves, labelled: _Reading/Lit Review_, _Research/Data_, and _Writing/Editing_
  - **Assessment points**: Place markers for _Poster_, _Proposal_, _Thesis_, and _Presentation_ at appropriate spots along the timeline \@Monica Jimenez Lloreda Can you please clarify for me if it is clear where exactly the assessment points will be on the image?
  - **Canvas alignment**: Include horizontal blocks or tabs underneath the timeline reflecting the module groupings listed above
  - **Parallel course bands**: Add thin horizontal strips to show when _Geoscience Communication_ and _Advanced Field Geoscience_ run concurrently
  - **Supervisor meetings**: Represent fortnightly check-ins with repeating markers (e.g. dots, flags, or similar)
- **Design tone and fidelity**
  At this stage, the team is looking for a **basic wireframe or draft layout**. Once that’s ready, they’ll provide feedback to refine emphasis, and structure but please use AU style guide colours and branding. If you could please let me know approx timeframe to create a basic wireframe so I can relay this back to the CBs.

‌

‌

---

‌

**Next step:**

Once I receive confirmation from you that the brief is clear for the media team, I’ll submit a formal ticket through the project management tool and request a timeline for the first iteration. I won’t be able to confirm dates or delivery timeframes until that ticket is submitted and triaged. (As you can see I’ve asked for clarification in my message)

Please let me know about the part highlighted in yellow and if you'd like me to make any adjustments to this before I send it out.

‌

Kind regards,
cards.name: Created brief for media team

cards.desc:
Hi Sophia,

‌

Thanks for forwarding this through.

‌

I’m happy to assist where I can, but just to flag—writing rubrics from scratch sits outside of my role as a Learning Designer. That said, I noticed the Canvas shell already includes rubric information attached to the assignments under Imported Assignments. Would Monica, Carl, or Stijn like me to copy and paste that rubric content into the Course Details form? I’m happy to assist with that admin task if it helps move things forward (see screenshot attached).

‌

I've also grouped the assignments into three categories—**Research Proposal, Research Report, and Research Thesis**—based on the milestone timeline graphic. However, I’m not entirely confident that each assignment has been placed in the most appropriate group, so it would be helpful if Monica or someone familiar with the assessment structure could review and confirm in Canvas (also see attached screenshot/s).

‌

Just to gently note, it is the Course Builder’s responsibility to complete the Course Details form, so if no edits have been made yet, there’s only so much I can do at this stage from a Learning Design perspective.

‌

Happy to help further where appropriate—just let me know.

Kindly,

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

**Online Programs Team | Learning Enhancement & Innovation**

cards.desc:
Dear Jane,

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **Advanced Microscopy Imaging and Analysis** course, scheduled for development from **17 March 2025** to **23 May 2025**. 

‌

My name is Rebecca McLachlan and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below).  

‌

**Course kick-off** 

Week 1: w/c 17 March 2025 

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design 

Week 4: w/c 7 April 2025 

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development 

Week 7: w/c 28 April 2025 

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development Completion 

Week 10: w/c 19 May 2025 

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_ 

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link**.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2690 "‌") 

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins. 

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌") 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌") 

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌") 

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Book an appointment [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-and-booking-consultations "‌") 

‌

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

[10am –12pm 13 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/gmEBCVARMkHx2GLwGHGf2UEKojU?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌") 

‌

[1 – 3pm 14 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/N_edCWLVNlF56Jg9JtxhwUouHr_?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌")  

‌

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌") 

‌

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below: 

‌

**Role** 

**Name** 

**Email** 

 **Support Provided** 

 **Time Allocation** 

**Available Timeframe** 

**Curriculum & Education Developer** 

‌

**(CED)** 

Chris Della Vedova 

[Chris.DellaVedova@unisa.edu.au](mailto:Chris.DellaVedova@unisa.edu.au "‌") 

Provides curriculum and pedagogical advice and support including course planning, assessments, course overview, and high-level module/topic structure.  

5 hours

15 hours

Provides guidance on selecting the best types of media for courses and assists with preparing for the development of a range of media types.

Monday 7 April – Friday 23 May

Supports developers to produce media content (through their team of skilled multimedia designers, professional recording studios, and self-serve facilities and equipment). 

15 hours

Support in identifying resources that align with your course. Copyright guidance. 

‌

As needed

‌

Monday 24 March – Friday 11 April  

**Academic Transition Panel**  

**(ATP)** 

Andrew MacKinnon (UoA), Emma Sherratt (UoA), Tom Raimondo (UniSA), & Craig Williams (UniSA) 

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development. 

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns.  

‌

‌

‌

‌

**Next Steps:**  

Next week is the first week of development and I encourage you to continue exploring the [Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌") with **Week 1: Onboarding.**  

‌

I look forward to seeing you at our kick-off meeting.  

‌

‌

Kind regards,

‌

**Bec McLachlan**

Hi Rich,

‌

Just updating you on the discussions I had with Alex regarding digital elab books. Alex, i believe plans to consider cadmus less from an academic integrity point but from a timeline tracking to see they're completing it mostly during class time and not leaving it until the end of the semester.

Cheers,

Sarah

Dear Ross, 

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **Classical Physics 2** course, scheduled for development from **17 March 2025** to **23 May 2025**. 

‌

My name is Rebecca McLachlan and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below).  

‌

**Course kick-off** 

Week 1: w/c 17 March 2025 

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design 

Week 4: w/c 7 April 2025 

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development 

Week 7: w/c 28 April 2025 

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development Completion 

Week 10: w/c 19 May 2025 

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_ 

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link**.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/2638 "‌") 

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins. 

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌") 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌") 

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌") 

‌

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Book an appointment [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/help-and-booking-consultations "‌") 

‌

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

[10am –12pm 13 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/S893Clx14mu21JV5KuGf5Uz7lur?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌") 

‌

[1 – 3pm 14 February](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/mmz-CmO54nijpwgKYUOh1URH4je?domain=events.teams.microsoft.com "‌")  

‌

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops?module_item_id=9793 "‌") 

‌

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, please reach out to book a consult for the weeks available below:

cards.name: ​​Applications in Next Generation Sequencing​​

cards.desc:
#### **Summary of work completed by LD (beyond LD remit)**

To support project continuity and maintain development momentum by week 4 of the 10-week cycle, the following tasks have been undertaken by me as LD, which would typically sit within the CED’s scope:

- **Curriculum mapping**
  Developed a full draft of the course map, structured into 3 curriculum modules and 10 topics, aligned to the course aim and CLOs.
- **Topic conceptualisation**
  Outlined key concepts for each topic, mapped to Canvas page structure (i.e. child pages), to ensure ease of content delivery planning and site build.
- **Assessment summary**
  Drafted a scaffolded, high-level assessment structure including task types, timing, CLO alignment, and estimated weightings, based on existing planning documents and informed by AI support.
- **Communication support**
  Drafted a detailed summary email to Course Developers explaining the intent and structure of the draft materials, while managing expectations around fidelity and constructive alignment.
- **Stakeholder coordination**
  Confirmed with Sarah (CED) that she will step in during Chris’s leave and help facilitate the next meeting, ensuring handover continuity and learner experience considerations are maintained.

---

#### **Outstanding work not yet completed by CD(s)**

As of week 4, the following tasks remain incomplete or require input/ownership from the Course Developers:

- **Confirmation or revision of CLO alignment**
  CLO-to-assessment and CLO-to-activity mapping has not been finalised by the CD(s), and feedback is required on whether the current CLOs adequately reflect intended learning and skill development.
- **Approval of course structure and sequencing**
  The proposed module/topic structure is yet to be confirmed or revised by the CD(s).
- **Detailed learning design per topic**
  Content development at the topic/concept level (e.g. readings, videos, quiz items, lab guides) is still outstanding.
- **Assessment rubric development**
  While partially outlined in existing documents, not all assessments have complete or finalised rubrics linked to CLOs or learning outcomes.
- **Aboriginal Knowledges integration**
  No direction has yet been provided on how Indigenous perspectives or content will be incorporated into the course.

cards.desc:

Cheers,

cards.name: Created Storyboard for 4 animations with CD, updated Media

cards.desc:
45 MINS

**Flashcard uplift – ethics in cybersecurity (ACS Code of Conduct)**

**Overview of changes:**

- Transformed a passive image-based H5P flashcard activity into an interactive, formative learning tool.
- Reworded the page content to **embed the exact answers used in the flashcards** within the explanatory text.
- Ensured alignment with the **Australian Computer Society (ACS) Code of Professional Conduct (2014)** using source language to maintain authenticity and clarity.
- Refined the structure of each card to include:
  - A **targeted question**
  - A **shortened, flexible answer** using keywords
  - A **tip** to prompt recall without revealing the answer

**Benefits for students:**

- Supports **active recall** rather than passive reading
- Reinforces key ethical concepts in a low-stakes, **self-check format**
- Embedding answer phrases directly in the content scaffolds learning and **improves accessibility**
- Builds confidence through **formative feedback** and guidance without relying on assessment pressure
- Clearer structure encourages **independent engagement** with professional values

**Benefits for teaching team:**

- Demonstrates alignment with ACS professional standards
- Encourages students to develop foundational understanding of professional ethics early
- Easy to adapt or replicate across other topics requiring applied ethical reasoning
cards.name: Security Principles - Ethics In Cybersecurity uplift page and H5P

cards.desc:
2 HRS

This revised H5P drag and drop activity represents a targeted uplift focused on clarity, consistency, and instructional integrity. The enhancements are designed to align more closely with learning outcomes while supporting an effective and fair learner experience.

- **Instructional clarity:** Updated directions and the addition of a workplace-based scenario help frame the task with practical relevance, encouraging applied thinking.
- **Refined phrasing of items:** Draggable items were reworded for consistency, ensuring each describes a clear, actionable task. This reduces ambiguity and supports more confident engagement with the content.
- **Terminology accuracy:** While early drafts used action-oriented phrasing for categories, these were later revised to retain standard cybersecurity terms (e.g. _Application Control_) based on academic guidance. This change ensures alignment with established discipline language.
- **Scenario-based context:** A concise, authentic scenario positions the activity in a workplace setting, supporting the application of theory to practice.
- **Improved interaction integrity:** All draggable items are now allowed to be dropped into any category box (rather than only their correct one), and answers are only validated on submission. This prevents students from using trial-and-error during the activity, preserving the integrity of the task as a formative learning opportunity.
- **Future adaptability and accessibility:** The structure of the revised activity lends itself to future enhancements, such as adaptive feedback, screen reader support, or scenario variations.

This uplift reflects a balance between pedagogical consistency, discipline-specific accuracy, and user experience, resulting in a stronger and more authentic learning activity.

Dear Georgina, 

‌

Thank you for supporting the development of the new Adelaide University **​Culture, Society and Climate Change​** course, scheduled for development from **6th January 2025** to**14th March 2025**.

‌

My name is Sally and I am a Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and am your key contact during course development. My support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones (dates below). 

‌

**Course kick-off**

Week 1: w/c 6th January

**Milestone 1 check-in:** Assessment and Course Design

Week 4: w/c 27th January

**Milestone 2 check-in:** Course Development

Week 7: w/c 17th February

**Milestone 3 check-in:** Course Development Completion

Week 10: w/c 10th March

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content.

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-development-key-information#development-process "‌")

**AU Course Development Essentials Site**

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete. 

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/enroll/HNY46D "‌")

**SharePoint Folder**

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work.

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌")(you can search by course name)

‌

**Training & Support**

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209/pages/training-hub "‌")

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.
