Support in identifying resources that align with your course. Copyright guidance. 

As needed 

**Recommended in Weeks 2 to 4:** 

‌

Monday 20 October – Friday 7 November 

**Academic Transition Panel**  

**(ATP)** 

Mahmud Masum (UoA) & Lan Snell (UniSA) 

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development. 

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns.  

‌

‌

**Curriculum Quality Panel**   

Curriculum Quality Panel members for each discipline oversee and lead the course quality review process. 

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link on myLearning** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link.](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/27618 "‌") 

**Curriculum Management System (CurV)** 

You have been granted the role of Designer in the Curriculum Management System, CurV.  

‌

Once you have finalised your Course Details Form you will use this to populate your course’s CurV record by the end of your 10-week development cycle. 

‌

Please use your a-number and password to log into CurV: [https://curriculum.adelaide.edu.au/cms](https://curriculum.adelaide.edu.au/cms "‌")   

‌

A Quick Reference Guide and video run through is available [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-details-form-slash-curv-population?module_item_id=665215 "‌"). 

‌

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins. 

[Course Developer Journey](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/key-information "‌") 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370 "‌") 

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") (you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions and workshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Learning Centre Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Learning Centre Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209 "‌") 

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Find out more about the library [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/library?module_item_id=665199 "‌") 

‌

You can contact the library at [library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌")  

**Course Design Workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

‌

The Course Design workshops will be held on the following dates: 

- Week 1: Thursday 1pm - 3pm  
- Week 1: Friday 10am - 12pm 

‌

Register for the Course Design workshop [here](https://events.humanitix.com/designing-your-course-and-assessments-workshop-online "‌") 

‌

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Library, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops-2 "‌") 

**Next Steps:**  

Next week is the first week of development and I encourage you to book in consults with your CED and continue thinking about your course design, you may wish to start populating the [Course Details Form](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-details-form-slash-curv-population?module_item_id=665215 "‌"). 

‌

I look forward to seeing you at our kick-off meeting.  

‌

Kind regards, 

**Sophia Carson**  

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

‌

**Integration Management Office (IMO)** 

Level 3, 108 North Terrace, Adelaide SA 5000 
E: [sophia.carson@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:sophia.carson@adelaide.edu.au "‌") 
 

[_**Creating a university for the future**_](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/q-XlC81VLxi6XMRr7InfXUy5U0M?domain=adelaideuni.edu.au "‌")

**Subject:** Re: Canvas Shell Structure Implemented for ARTI 6000

‌

Hi Orvila,

In the process of adding content from your PPTX into Topic 2, it’s become clear that creating a separate concept page for each PPTX slide results in too many pages within the Canvas shell. This layout could feel overwhelming for students and make navigation difficult. See below (I will delete these pages, I just wanted to show you first hand)

![Screenshot 2025-11-12 at 3.56.09 pm.png](cid:963755869*7399F661-B28E-492E-BCBC-BFB63972F8DA)

As I’m not the subject matter expert, I can’t determine which PPTX topics could be merged without risking content accuracy, and doing a detailed mapping exercise would be quite time-consuming for both of us.

I’d suggest instead embedding each PPTX on a single concept page, with additional teaching or extended explanations placed on separate concept pages where necessary. This would streamline the student experience while still allowing space for deeper material.

I’ll send you a calendar invite for a quick discussion before 2 pm on Thursday to confirm the best approach.

Kind regards,

**Rich Bartlett**

cards.desc:
Dear Judith, 

‌

Welcome to the official development period for **Radiation Biology, Protection and Epidemiology** course, scheduled for development from **8 September 2025** to **14 November 2025**. This course has been selected by the Academic Transition Panel (ATP) for at-elbow support. 

‌

I am sending this on behalf of Vy Gubatina, your Project Officer within the Curriculum team in the Integration Management Office (IMO) and she is your key contact during course development. Vy’s support will include ensuring that you have access to everything you need and are aware of all the resources you have available to you. She will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss your progress against the key development milestones. 

‌

**Links and Helpful Resources** 

Resource 

Description 

Link 

**Course Shell Link** 

This is the course shell where you will be building out the content. 

[Course Shell Link**.**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567 "‌") 

**Curriculum Management System (CurV)** 

You have been granted the role of Designer in the Curriculum Management System, CurV.  

‌

Once you have finalised your Course Details Form you will use this to populate your course’s CurV record by the end of your 10-week development cycle. 

‌

Please use your a-number and password to log into CurV: [https://curriculum.adelaide.edu.au/cms](https://curriculum.adelaide.edu.au/cms "smartCard-inline")    

‌

A Quick Reference Guide and video run through is available [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/course-details-form-slash-curv-population?module_item_id=665215 "‌"). 

‌

**Course Development Journey** 

Provides guidance on how to structure your work across the 10 weeks and I will refer to this in our milestone check-ins.  

Attached document 

**AU Course Development Essentials Site** 

This site outlines key activities, direction for the Design, Development and Build phases and key information on the Adelaide Attainment Model and important templates you must complete.  

‌

As you are receiving dedicated support via the At Elbow model, the hours for TALAS support and consult booking section within the Essential Site are not applicable to you. 

[Adelaide University Course Development Essentials site](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370 "‌") 

‌

**SharePoint Folder** 

You have a dedicated folder for your course in the AU Course Development SharePoint. This holds your documents related to course development and is a collaborative space for you, other developers and assigned TALAS team members. Documents saved in SharePoint can be worked on live and will auto-save, allowing you to have visibility of the latest document version, so it is important that you use this folder for all work. 

[Part 3: Course Development (sharepoint.com)](https://adelaideuni.sharepoint.com/sites/aupcd/SitePages/Part-3--Course-Development.aspx "‌") 

 (you can search by course name) 

‌

**Training & Support** 

‌

_We strongly encourage you to attend training sessions andworkshops and view the asynchronous training available to you to support you in the design and build of your course._ 

**Training Hub:** The AU Canvas Learning Centre Training Hub contains links to live and asynchronous training resources. 

[Training Hub](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/209 "‌") 

**Library:** a dedicated team is available to support you in selecting content for your Reading List and using the Course Readings tool.  

Find out more about the library [here](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/library?module_item_id=665199 "‌") 

‌

You can contact the library at [library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌")  

**Course Design workshop:** This 2-hour online design workshop will outline course design and allow time for small group activities to work on developing your course rubric and assessments. 

‌

The Course Design workshops will be held on the following dates: 

- Week 1: Thursday 1pm - 3pm  

- Week 1: Friday 10am - 12pm 

‌

Register for the Course Design workshop [here](https://url.au.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/6unLCOMKB2ip4jGEWtEf7tGfamn?domain=events.humanitix.com "‌") 

‌

**Workshops:** Access to register for various workshops, such as Canvas, Media, Assessment Building. 

[Workshops](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/370/pages/workshops-2 "‌") 

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

During the 10-week Course Development process you have access to support from **TALAS** (an amalgamation of **TIU** - Teaching Innovation Unit and **LEI** - Learning Enhancement and Innovation) and the **Library**. Below are the key contacts for your support, who will support you throughout the 10 weeks. We will meet regularly together and you are also encouraged to meet outside of this as well to progress work. 

‌

**Role** 

**Name** 

**Contact Details** 

**Support Provided** 

**Time Allocation** 

**Curriculum & Education Developer**  

**(CED)** 

Sarah Davey

[Sarah.Davey@unisa.edu.au](mailto:Sarah.Davey@unisa.edu.au "‌")

CEDs provide pedagogical advice and support in curriculum andassessment design. This includes reviewing and providing feedback on course, assignment, and rubric design. 

10 

**Learning Designer**  

**(LD)** 

Richard Bartlett

[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌")  

Provides advice and support in the conceptualisation, design, andsequencing of course content andlearning activities. They can help you scaffold learning activities toward assessment, scope anddesign the development of learning content, and provide advice andsupport in the effective use of technology in learning andteaching. 

‌

20

Gulshan Gupta

[gulshan.gupta@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:gulshan.gupta@adelaide.edu.au "‌")

Provides build consultation andspecialist learning experience advice. Contributes technical build support to the At-Elbow team including; development of key learning assets, setup of enterprise external tool assessments, alignment of content with accessibility and usability standards, and population into Canvas. DEDs also develop design briefs in consultation with TALAS Media. 

‌

30 

**Media Design** 

‌

Jeremy Bowtell 

[jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au](mailto:jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au "‌") 

Provides advice and support in selecting types of multimedia for your course, exploring options for filming videos, scheduling filming or media development, and designing bespoke media assets. 

‌

10

**Media Production** 

‌

Jeremy Bowtell 

[jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au](mailto:jeremy.bowtell@unisa.edu.au "‌") 

Supports developers to produce engaging media content (through their team of skilled multimedia designers, filmmakers, animators, recording studios, self-serve facilities and equipment).    

20 

‌

**Library** 

(course readings andresource selection) 

[library@adelaideuni.edu.au](mailto:library@adelaideuni.edu.au "‌") 

Support in identifying resources that align with your course. Copyright guidance. 

‌

‌

As needed 

**Academic Transition Panel**  

**(ATP)** 

Andrew MacKinnon (UoA), Emma Sherratt (UoA), Tom Raimondo (UniSA), & Craig Williams (UniSA)

Discipline specific academic leads for AU Course Development. 

They provide the programmatic approach and are the contact for Course Aim, CLO amendment requests or workload concerns. 

‌

‌

You can continue with the design work you have been doing. We look forward to seeing you at our first meeting next week. You may wish to contact your CED to organise a time asap next week.   

‌

‌

Kind Regards,

‌

**Eileen Cheong** (she/her) on behalf of **Vy Gubatina** 

Project Officer | Program & Course Development | Curriculum

cards.desc:
Hi Judith and Tyron,

I’ve now set up the [assignment pages](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/assignments "‌") in Canvas and aligned the assessment structure with the course outline. The assessment groups currently in place are:

![screencapture-learn-adelaide-edu-au-courses-7567-assignments-2025-11-10-14\_43\_48.png](cid:BDF50D07-E394-49E2-B98B-247608B47FB5)

- **Quiz Portfolio** – 10%
- **Problem Sets** _(Please update this term ‘problem set’ if not appropriate)_ – 40% total (four problem-solving tasks, 10% each)
- **Examination** – 50%

Each "Problem Set" is numbered and titled to match course details form:

      2.1: Radiation sources, risks and biological effects

      2.2: Health effects, shielding and protection

      2.3: Tumour development and modelling

      2.4: Legislation and epidemiology

‌

I’ve also updated the **Course Roadmap** [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/pages/course-roadmap](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/pages/course-roadmap "‌") to show maintaining a two-week turnaround between tasks (as Judith lauded to in our meeting this afternoon), and aligning topic names, forums etc with the module structure in purple.

![screencapture-learn-adelaide-edu-au-courses-7567-pages-course-roadmap-2025-11-10-14\_55\_23.png](cid:341986953*253C182B-42E6-4B6F-9AF3-06FACF55AE9C)

‌

When you have a chance, could you please:

1. Review individual each **assignment page** for wording and clarity. \@Tyron Turnbull you can open each assignment by clicking on each page here: [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/assignments](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/7567/assignments "‌")
   1. I’ve left some red highlights on the the pages for one of you to please edit and update. If the wording is not in sync with your expectations please feel free to change entirely. 
   1. ![Screenshot 2025-11-10 at 3.04.02 pm.png](cid:341986953*74BC3AE7-DCE2-4249-BD79-7E1748ED0DBD)
2. Confirm that the **grouping and weightings** match your expectations.
3. Review the **Course Roadmap** and make any adjustments you’d like.

Hopefully this is helpful and 80-90% of the way there in terms of correct wording, due dates, percentages etc. I imagine it’s not perfect so please do take the take to go through the pages and edit them directly and this should be fairly easy. If you need help with editing these page in Canvas please don’t hesitate to reach out

Thanks,

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

**Online Programs Team | Learning Enhancement & Innovation**
Division of Academic and Student Engagement

### Content and learning resources

- Content migration into the course underway, with support expected from the Course Builders team.

- Noted that a new dynamic homepage will be available soon, which should enhance the student experience.

---

**In summary:**
The major elements of course structure, assessment alignment, and rubrics are in place. Content migration and media development are progressing with support from Course Builders and Media. The homepage is being worked on to fit the standard template until the dynamic version is released. Outstanding items include confirming invigilated exam options and continuing resource development (H5P, videos).

Dear Anna; Elysia and Joanne, 

‌

As you are aware, **Introduction to Health Research** has been scheduled for additional development from in Cycle 11, **4 August 2025** to **10 October 2025.** Following on from the peer review and finalisation period, there are many items to be completed by the end of this additional development period which will be discussed in detail at our meeting next week. 

‌

As your Project Officer, I will meet with you periodically throughout the 10-week development cycle to discuss progress. 

‌

**Additional Development Discussion** 

Week 1: w/c 4 August 2025 

**Milestone 1 check-in** 

Week 4: w/c 25 August 2025 

**Milestone 2 check-in** 

Week 7: w/c 15 September 2025 

**Milestone 3 check-in** 

Week 10: w/c 6 October 2025 

_(If you are aware of any leave or scheduled absences during the above weeks, please let me know ASAP.)_ 

‌

**Key Contacts:** 

The TALAS support available during this additional development period is outlined below. The timeline of support is the same as standard course development, but the time allocation has reduced. 

‌

**Role** 

**Name** 

**Email** 

 **Support Provided** 

 **Time Allocation** 

**Available Timeframe**

‌

**(LD)** 

Richard Bartlett 

[rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au](mailto:rich.bartlett@adelaide.edu.au "‌") 

Contributes to the identification and design of media and learning activities (for face-to-face and online formats). They may also help to extend the course plan. 

7\.5 hours 

**Weeks 4 to 8:** 

‌

Monday 25 August – Friday 26 September 

‌

\*Please note, both your CED & LD are available during week 4 

**Course Build Support** 

Your Learning Designer will arrange course build support 

Builds some content in the LMS; completion of fundamental ad-hoc design tasks. 

‌

10 hours

‌

Monday 8 September  – Friday 10 October

cards.desc:
Hi Bonnie,

Quick update: I have a basic **Google Form + QR code** setup which live and working as intended.

- Students enter their name and email, submit, and are **automatically allocated a topic**.
- The script then **emails each student** their allocated topic immediately.
- I’ve set **it so the script for** each topic has a fixed capacity (currently 30). Once a topic fills, it’s no longer assigned.
- All allocations are visible in the **linked Google Sheet** for academic oversight.

Links for testing:

- Form: [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSclqRhjO2vq5aOOVQLzTo1ywwuf8Oi1tJXj-K5jMUKOWypn8A/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=106757145152036440538](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSclqRhjO2vq5aOOVQLzTo1ywwuf8Oi1tJXj-K5jMUKOWypn8A/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=106757145152036440538&utm_source=chatgpt.com "‌")
- Responses sheet (live log): [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WJzkIdNzNV9ZsUOa76XvcU5ClM\_weN5bq\_fqa8G0jkI/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WJzkIdNzNV9ZsUOa76XvcU5ClM_weN5bq_fqa8G0jkI/edit?usp=sharing&utm_source=chatgpt.com "‌")

I’ve also included the **QR code** so you can test it. Functionally, if this meets the need, we can uplift the wording and visual style next.

**Rich Bartlett**
Learning Designer FHEA

- Need help with writing instructions for assessment
  - CBs to be explicit in instructions
- Challenge of sequencing “everything”,
  - Traditionally, students do a weekly then, then a prac report, the assessment is the summation of the prac reports > CHANGE is now that report is worth 10% and students have an extra week to obtain feedback.

  - **They want students to make use of the feedback**

cards.name: Honours Plant, Food and Soil Sciences Project - Han and Cristian

cards.desc:
**Summary of engagement:**

- Cristian Varela (Honours Director) and Han Weng contacted me about converting the _Honours Handbook_ PDF into an interactive H5P object for AU Learn, to be used in both the _Advanced Plant, Food and Soil Sciences_ and _Honours Plant, Food and Soil Sciences Project_ courses.
- Key requirement: exclude all timetable dates (updated every 6 months) and retain the rest of the content.

**What’s being asked:**

- Provide a concept and prototype for an interactive version of the handbook in H5P, suitable for embedding in both courses.
- Recommend appropriate H5P content types to make the handbook engaging and easy to navigate.

**What I’ve done so far:**

- Reviewed the PDF and extracted headings and content (excluding timetable dates).
- Created an **H5P Interactive Book** mock-up in my sandpit with the handbook structure and content copied verbatim from the PDF.
- Developed a **conversion plan** outlining potential H5P content types for each section (e.g., Information Wall, Accordion, Timeline, Documentation Tool).
- Added Cristian to my sandpit for review and feedback.
- Offered to meet via Teams (tomorrow, Friday morning, or next week) to discuss direction and refinements.
cards.name: AFW Honours Handbook – H5P Interactive Book prototype

cards.name: Overview

cards.desc:
Hi Tarikul,

‌

Thanks for your time this afternoon.

‌

After we got off the call, I starting mapping the Module and topics named to the canvas module boxes. But I hit a road block for week 2 & 3 because it seems as thought you’ve put multiple _**concept**_ title pages into the cell instead of just a single _**topic** name_**.**

As you know the naming convention is module name : topic name with subpages nested underneath being “concepts”

‌

With topic 2 for example, I’ve created all the items in that cell table from the course details document as concept pages.

‌

‌

‌

If you could please let me know what you would like topic/week 2 and 3 to be named I can update this.

‌

Further to this Tarikul, do you have the names/order of the concepts pages prepared for each topic/week?

‌

‌

‌

I have also replicated the structure for the Module Overview pages from Global Business Analysis

‌

‌

I’ll wait to hear from you.

‌

Kindly,
cards.name: Created course structure

cards.desc:
Hi Tarikul,

Hope you’ve settled back in smoothly after your time away.

I just wanted to follow up on the updates I shared last week regarding the reading list integration and the creation of separate “Watch” pages from the TY/media links you’d added. Let me know if you had a chance to look over those, and whether you're happy with how they’ve been framed.

Also, just checking in to see if you need any support from me this week or next—whether with reviewing content, finalising topic elements, or collaborating on any design adjustments. More than happy to assist or make refinements where needed.

Feel free to reply here or tag me on any Canvas page if something needs attention.

Kindly,
Rich
cards.name: Sent follow up email

cards.desc:
Hi Tarikul.

‌

Happy Monday and hope you had a great weekend!

‌

I have been through your course, right through from Topic 1 to 10. When you have time you may wish to review my suggestions. If you’re not content with the changes the options are:

‌

1. Easily revert back to your last edit via “page history” and restore the page with no uplifts implemented.
2. If you deem the page is an absolute improvement then no changes required
3. If you consider a page a _slight_ improvement however you believe edits are required
   1. This will require you to go on to the page, click edit and make the changes. 

‌

The suggested uplifts I’ve implemented are mainly aesthetic such as wrapping information in direction boxes, or providing framing text for videos, activities. Also fixing US spelling to AU/British.

‌

I’ve will add PDF version of this reflections pages so students can download it and print. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/course-concepts-and-reflections-summary-table](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/course-concepts-and-reflections-summary-table "‌")

**Please note:** Unfortunately, there is no further scope or time unfortunately to further edit the content pages. Please make any final refinements as you see fit.

‌

That said, there are some easy and specific fixes I’m happy to help fix if you can provide the supporting material:

- **Topic 5 overview** is missing text. Please input this directly. [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/topic-5-overview-2?module\_item\_id=613596](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/topic-5-overview-2?module_item_id=613596 "‌")
- Can you please clarify what you mean by “**Concept video descriptor” on this page> (What is the intended stimulus for the students?)**[**https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/topic-6-overview?module\_item\_id=550689**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/topic-6-overview?module_item_id=550689 "‌")
  - **Flagging, once I know I can update this page: **[**https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/topic-7-overview?module\_item\_id=551330#:~:text=Concept%20video%20descriptor%3A**](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/topic-7-overview?module_item_id=551330#:~:text=Concept%20video%20descriptor%3A "‌")
    - [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/topic-8-overview?module\_item\_id=551337#:~:text=Script%20for%20the%20Concept%20Video%3A](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/topic-8-overview?module_item_id=551337#:~:text=Script%20for%20the%20Concept%20Video%3A "‌")
- Course conclusion info missing: [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/course-conclusion?module\_item\_id=376925](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/4800/pages/course-conclusion?module_item_id=376925 "‌")

‌

Kindly,

cards.desc:
2\.5 HRS

Hi Anna and Joe,

Thanks for this extra info and pointing me in the right direction to the content in your sharepoint.

I’ve created prototype module sequences for both ‘**Merits Review’** and ‘**Procedural Fairness’**. These include structured concept pages, sample interactive elements, and APA-style reference formatting at the bottom of each page to give you a sense of how a topic design could function.

‌

I've referred to the course details document, lecture slides and materials you've shared and used AI prompting in some instances to write HTML code and help structure and scaffold each topic. Please note, the name of the **concept pages** and the **text** currently on the pages is ‘dummy content’ — think of it like lorem ipsum, except more relevant to the subject. While it may resemble what you intend to include, the final wording will need to come from you both. I’ve used this placeholder content to maintain coherence and avoid confusion from irrelevant subject matter.

‌

I’ve also embedded some images from your lecture slides that may or may not be reused — we can flag later if you’d like any of them recreated or uplifted by the media team.

‌

The purpose of this prototype is to:

- Show my interpretation of good practice in instructional design
- Demonstrate the use of H5P interactives and DesignPlus callout boxes
- Provide a consistent and accessible student experience through logical sequencing and academic styling

‌

Please treat this as a foundation for discussion when we meet next week. If anything looks off or isn’t what you had in mind, please let me know as soon as possible so I can make the necessary corrections early.

‌

You can start exploring the modules here:

‌

- Merits Review module:
  [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582/pages/module-overview-5?module\_item\_id=581505](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582/pages/module-overview-5?module_item_id=581505 "‌")
- Procedural Fairness module:
  [https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582/pages/module-overview-2?module\_item\_id=581500](https://learn.adelaide.edu.au/courses/5582/pages/module-overview-2?module_item_id=581500 "‌")

Just use the **‘Next’** button in the bottom-right corner of each page to step through the full sequence.

**Final note:** After our meeting next week, my job will be to set up the concept page titles based on your advice and direction, and I’m happy to continue structuring the pages accordingly. The idea is to set you both up for success by creating a “paint-by-numbers” framework — once that’s in place, I’ll work behind the scenes to uplift other content, create the H5P interactives, and liaise with the media team. I just wanted to be clear that you’ll need to guide me and map the actual learning content.

Looking forward to your feedback.

Kindly,

**Rich Bartlett**
cards.name: Created Prototype of 2 topics

cards.desc:
Hi Joe and Anna,

‌

Thanks again for your time this morning.

‌

After you left the meeting, Joe, Anna and I made a few small tweaks to the titles on the graphic to improve clarity and alignment, which Anna will also email you about. We then discussed the idea of uplifting this visual into a short animated video with voiceover to step students through the decision-making process. The goal would be to provide a clear and engaging asynchronous resource that demystifies the pathways available for review.

‌

I’ve used AI to flesh out a rough outline and draft script that shows how either one of you—or someone else—could narrate the content. It’s designed to closely follow the visual structure of the graphic and frame the learning experience in a clear and student-friendly way.

‌

Please let me know if it’s done a somewhat accurate job in capturing how you would create a such a script/structure. If you’d like to go down this path, please make changes or annotations that would help bring it into alignment with what’s needed to ensure it’s comprehensive and accurate from a content perspective.

‌

Looking forward to your thoughts.

‌

‌

‌

‌

‌

Kindly,

‌
